title: Enforce default accelerator conflict policy at startup
body:
Manual salvage for #436 after the autonomous queue repeatedly stalled in codex-exec.

What changed:
- Documents `editor-ui::menus::default_menu` as the owner of default accelerators and records the fatal/default vs non-fatal/runtime conflict policy.
- Adds `assert_default_accelerators_conflict_free()` in `editor-ui`; it resolves a supplied registry with `PredicateContext::default()` and panics with shortcut + entry ids only when conflicts exist.
- Calls the helper once in `EguiHost::new()` immediately after `default_editor_menu()` builds the canonical startup registry.
- Appends task 171 as the required post-keybinding-policy source/docs-read-only audit.

Verification run locally:
- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo test -p rge-editor-ui`
- `cargo test -p rge-editor-egui-host`
- `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell`
- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all`
- `git diff --check`

Canonical gate note:
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` passes fmt + architecture lints + architecture-lint tests, then fails at `cargo deny check` because `memmap2 0.9.10` now has RUSTSEC-2026-0186 and must be updated to `>=0.9.11`. That is outside #436/task-170's allowed Cargo surface, so this PR leaves it untouched.
- GitHub Actions checks currently fail before any recorded steps or runner assignment, so their red state is not giving code-level logs yet.

Refs #436.

body:
Establish default-accelerator ownership and fatal startup enforcement for default accelerator conflicts only; runtime conflict handling remains non-fatal and unchanged.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-ui/src/menus/default_menu.rs`, `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`, and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`.
Done: a pure editor-ui helper panics with clear offending shortcuts on default conflicts, `EguiHost::new()` invokes it once, tests cover real-menu zero conflicts and injected duplicate, accelerator values stay unchanged at 19/zero conflicts.
MUST NOT edit per-keystroke routing, shortcut conflict display, Command/predicate logic, CAD/editor-actions/editor-state/Cargo/schemas/workflows/scripts/automation; if enforcement needs a MUST-NOT surface, record `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED` instead.

**Self-re-arm (final step, required):**
After implementation and verification, APPEND exactly one bounded source/docs-read-only
AUDIT task as task 171 â€” a "Post-keybinding-policy Phase 9 next-task source audit"
mirroring the task-169 audit block: confirm default-accelerator conflicts are fatal at
the single host startup site, the per-keystroke/non-fatal display paths are unchanged,
no accelerator value changed (table = 19, zero conflicts), and that only editor-ui
(enforcement + tests) and the editor-egui-host startup call site changed. Task 171 is
docs/source-read-only (its `MAY edit` includes `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, `Status.md`,
`HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`; it MUST NOT edit Rust source, tests, or
automation). Task 171's final step appends the next bounded FEATURE task (or, if none is
in-policy, records a single `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a
"Recommendation for human approval" block). Copy this Self-re-arm requirement verbatim
into the task 171 block you author. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

title: AI dispatch idle: brief dry, self-re-arm chain may have broken

labels.name: needs-human
labels.description: AI dispatch paused for human review/decision

body:
The autonomous dispatch loop selected no task and the brief carries no `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED:` marker. Under the self-re-arm protocol the brief should never run dry on its own, so this usually means the last dispatched task did not append its next task (a broken chain), or all planned work is genuinely complete.

Review `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` and either append the next task to re-arm the loop or unarm it deliberately, then close this issue.

Review `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` and either append the next task to re-arm the loop or unarm it deliberately, then close this issue.

title: Add Delete Current CAD Cuboid menu affordance

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Add one explicit no-shortcut Edit-menu command `DeleteCurrentCadCuboid` that routes to the existing headless `EditorShell::delete_current_cad_cuboid` path.
Scope is limited to editor-ui menu command/predicate/default-menu/tests, editor-shell `render_path.rs` + lifecycle predicate/tests, egui-host menu/shortcut test fixtures, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, and generated dispatch artifacts.
Share the exact tracked-CAD selection rule between menu enablement and routing; do not modify `commands.rs`, editor-actions, CAD crates, Cargo files, Delete/Cut behavior, or egui-host rendering/projection logic.
Done when the menu and command palette expose "Delete Current CAD Cuboid", enabled only for the exact single tracked CAD selection, with warn-and-swallow rejection and all listed verification passing.

**Self-re-arm (final step, required) - BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):**
After implementation and verification, APPEND exactly one bounded
source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 167 - an audit of the menu-affordance CAD
delete boundary (the new `Command::DeleteCurrentCadCuboid` routes ONLY to
`delete_current_cad_cuboid`; its menu enablement and the route guard share the ONE
exact-tracked-CAD rule; rejection is warn-and-swallow with no fallback/stack
growth; the existing `Command::Delete`, Delete-key accelerator, Cut, and
wrapper-world delete are unchanged; `editor-actions` stays generic; the
`DeleteCurrentCadCuboid` action + `delete_current_cad_cuboid` entry point are
unchanged; editor-egui-host rendering/projection logic is unchanged with only its
`menu_tests.rs` / `shortcut_help.rs` fixtures updated for the new entry). Task 167
is a GATED audit: it MUST
NOT append task 168 or any feature task, and its final step MUST record the next
`NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a "Recommendation for
human approval" block (proposed next feature, exact edit surface, risks,
verification, why smallest). Carry this gated-audit instruction verbatim into the
task 167 block it authors. Task 166 itself MUST NOT directly record a
`NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED` marker UNLESS it cannot safely append the task 167 audit -
appending task 167 is the required primary outcome. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
to do this.

title: Route menu Delete to tracked CAD cuboid delete

Route existing `Command::Delete` to `EditorShell::delete_current_cad_cuboid()` only when the current entity selection is exactly the shell-tracked `cad_entity`; all empty, non-CAD, and mixed selections must preserve existing wrapper-world Delete behavior, and `Command::Cut` stays unchanged.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, and generated current-dispatch artifacts; do not edit `editor-actions`, CAD crates, UI/host crates, Cargo, workflows, schemas, or automation.
Done: CAD delete route is selected only for exact single tracked-CAD selection; rejected stale/invalid CAD preflight logs/swallows without fallback, selection clear, or bus-stack growth; tests cover CAD route, undo/redo, stale render/selection cleanup, dirty/save cursor, non-CAD/mixed/empty/stale cases, and existing wrapper Delete/Cut behavior.
Verify the listed focused `rge-editor-shell` filters, `cargo check -p rge-editor-actions -p rge-editor-shell`, fmt, editor-actions no-CAD-reference grep, `git diff --name-only`, `git diff --check`, and task-heading grep.

source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 165 - an audit of the menu-Delete CAD

body:
Implement headless `EditorShell::delete_current_cad_cuboid` as the shell-only inverse of task 160: submit immutable `DeleteCurrentCadCuboid` through the existing generic CommandBus context; do not edit `editor-actions` or the action contract.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, and generated current-dispatch handoff/log artifacts.
Done: apply deletes the tracked single cuboid via restore-to-empty-parent + despawn + tick + clear `cad_entity` + selection prune; undo restores from_head with a fresh tracked entity; redo deletes again; invalid states reject with no bus-stack growth.
Verify targeted delete/undo/redo/reject tests plus existing add/cleanup/inspection filters, `cargo check -p rge-editor-actions -p rge-editor-shell`, fmt, generic-context guard `rg`, `git diff --name-only`, and `git diff --check`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required) - BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):** After implementation and verification, APPEND exactly one bounded source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 163 - an audit of the post-delete CAD-mutation boundary (delete routed through CommandBus, editor-actions still generic, shell-owned tracked-entity lifecycle coherent across add+delete+undo+redo). Task 163 is a GATED audit: it MUST NOT append task 164 or any feature task, and its final step MUST record the next `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a "Recommendation for human approval" block (proposed next feature, exact edit surface, risks, verification, why smallest). Carry this gated-audit instruction verbatim into the task 163 block it authors. Task 162 itself MUST NOT directly record a `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED` marker UNLESS it cannot safely append the task 163 audit - appending task 163 is the required primary outcome. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

title: Route first CAD cuboid add through CommandBus

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Widen `editor-actions` with a minimal generic action-context contract while preserving object-safe `Box<dyn Action>`, existing World-only behavior, coalescing, ledger, dirty/save-mark, and no CAD/editor-shell deps.
In `editor-shell`, define the CAD action/context and route `EditorShell::add_cad_cuboid_to_empty_scene` through `command_bus.submit`; keep scope to editor-actions + editor-shell lifecycle tests, with no cad-core/cad-projection/render/UI/Cargo changes.
Done means bus-routed cuboid add, undo, and redo work; revert restores pre-add graph/projection/world state with no stale entity/mesh, existing empty-scene rejection and stale-id normalization still hold, and apply-time undo metadata is handled without unsafe/global state.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required) - BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):**
After implementation and verification, APPEND exactly one bounded
source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 161 - an audit of the post-bus-routed
first-CAD-mutation boundary. Task 161 is a GATED audit: it MUST NOT append task
162 or any feature task, and its final step MUST record the next
`NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a "Recommendation for
human approval" block (proposed next feature, exact edit surface, risks,
verification, why smallest). Carry this gated-audit instruction into the task
161 block it authors. Task 160 itself MUST NOT directly record a

title: Wire stale CAD cleanup into cuboid-add preflight

body:
Call `EditorShell::clear_stale_tracked_cad_entity` inside `add_cad_cuboid_to_empty_scene` before the existing `cad_entity` empty-scene guard, so stale tracked-id-only residue can be normalized without widening the first-cuboid boundary.
Scope is `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`; do not edit CAD core/projection, CommandBus/actions, render path, UI/host, Cargo, workflows, or automation.
Done: stale-id-only shells can add the first cuboid; live tracked CAD entities and any installed graph/projection/world/render/uploaded state still reject as `SceneNotEmpty`; cleanup remains liveness-only and clears only `self.cad_entity`.
Self-re-arm (final step, required) - BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):
After implementation and verification, append exactly one bounded source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 159 - an audit of the post-stale-id preflight-normalization boundary. Task 159 is a GATED audit: it MUST NOT append task 160 or any feature/implementation task, and MUST NOT self-re-arm with a follow-up feature. Instead, task 159's final step MUST record its conclusion as a single `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker line immediately followed by a `Recommendation for human approval` block covering proposed next feature, exact edit surface, risks, verification, and why it is the smallest coherent next step. The autonomous driver then detects the marker, files a `needs-human` review issue, and pauses for operator approval. Task 159 must require dispatcher-provided GitHub-state snapshot evidence for queue/already-filed-task claims, compare current source, and carry this same gated-audit instruction (record NEEDS_HUMAN, do NOT append a feature) into the task 159 block it authors. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

title: AI dispatch NEEDS_HUMAN: stale tracked-CAD cleanup caller approval

labels.name: needs-human
labels.description: AI dispatch paused for human review/decision

body:
The autonomous dispatch loop recorded a NEEDS_HUMAN boundary in `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` after task 157 and cannot self-re-arm without a human decision.

Recorded marker:
NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: 2026-06-16 - stale tracked-CAD cleanup helper is implemented and tested, but approving its first production caller needs a human decision

Recommendation for human approval:
- Proposed next feature: wire `EditorShell::clear_stale_tracked_cad_entity` into the existing `add_cad_cuboid_to_empty_scene` empty-scene preflight so a stale stored `cad_entity` alone can be cleared before the current first-cuboid add guard runs, while preserving the existing rejection for installed CAD graph/projection/world state, render-only content, and other partial/non-empty scenes.
- Exact edit surface: `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs` and `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`; use `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` only if an adjacent lifecycle export or route adjustment is strictly required; generated current-dispatch handoff/audit artifacts only.
- Risks: the helper currently has no production caller; routing it through UI, CommandBus, dirty/save, graph restore, projection despawn, render refresh, mesh upload, or GPU paths would expand authority beyond the cleanup primitive. The approved feature should only normalize stale tracked-id state before the existing add preflight and should not turn render-mesh absence into a cleanup predicate.
- Verification: run focused `clear_stale_tracked_cad_entity` and `cad_scene_inspection` lifecycle tests, add a route-specific lifecycle test for stale-id-only preflight normalization, run `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib`, `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`, the lifecycle/projection evidence greps from this audit, `git diff --name-only`, and `git diff --check`.
- Why this is the smallest coherent next step: current source already has a narrow stale-id cleanup primitive and tests, and `rg` shows it is not wired to a production caller. The proposed follow-up decides one caller boundary without changing graph/projection cleanup policy, render behavior, CommandBus/undo/dirty/save authority, UI routing, CAD-core, or cad-projection.

Local gate state:
- `origin/main` contains ISSUE-412 commit `5f39c96`.
- `.ai/dispatch.auto-halt` is present and pauses subsequent Auto ticks.
- No task 158 heading was appended.

This issue was filed manually after verification because the local halt sentinel claimed a needs-human issue had been filed, but GitHub had no open `needs-human` issue for this boundary.

title: Clear stale tracked-CAD entity ids

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Add `EditorShell::clear_stale_tracked_cad_entity(&mut self) -> bool` (or tighter local name) in `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`/`tests.rs` (`mod.rs` only for adjacent lifecycle placement) to clear only stale `self.cad_entity` ids against live CAD-world `BRepHandle` liveness.
Return `true` only when a previously stored stale id is cleared; return `false` for fresh/no tracked id/live tracked id; do not despawn, tick projection, restore/rollback graph state, mutate selection/camera, enqueue actions, mark dirty/saved, save/load/open/close, initialize render state, upload meshes, touch GPU, or edit forbidden surfaces.
Tests must cover fresh shell no-op, successful first cuboid add no-op, `restore_to(pre_add_head)` plus `despawn_brep_entity` stale cleanup where inspection reports present true/live false before helper and present false after, while `cad_scene_inspection` remains read-only and consistent.
Run the focused `rge-editor-shell` tests/check/fmt and greps named in task 156, including no diff under `crates/editor-actions/**`, plus `git diff --name-only` and `git diff --check`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required) â€” BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):** After the implementation is complete and verified, append exactly one bounded source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 157 - an audit of the post-stale-tracked-CAD-cleanup boundary.
Task 157 is a GATED audit: it MUST NOT append task 158 or any feature/implementation task, and MUST NOT self-re-arm with a follow-up feature. Instead, task 157's final step MUST record its conclusion as a single `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker line immediately followed by a "Recommendation for human approval" block (proposed next feature, exact edit surface, risks, verification, and why it is the smallest coherent next step).
The autonomous driver then detects the marker, files a `needs-human` review issue, and pauses for operator approval - the loop does NOT auto-ship another feature. Task 157 must require dispatcher-provided GitHub-state snapshot evidence for queue/already-filed-task claims, compare current source, and carry this same gated-audit instruction (record NEEDS_HUMAN, do NOT append a feature) into the task 157 block it authors. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

---

title: Add tracked-CAD liveness to CadSceneInspection

body:
Extend the read-only headless `EditorShell` CAD inspection snapshot with tracked-CAD entity liveness/render-mesh fields computed from existing `cad_entity`, `cad_world`, `BRepHandle`, and `render_mesh_for` state.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` if needed, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, and generated dispatch artifacts.
Do not mutate CAD graph/projection/world, selection, camera, render assets, GPU state, CommandBus, dirty/save state, UI, render path, Cargo, workflows, or CAD/projection crates.
Done: focused lifecycle tests cover fresh shell, first cuboid add, rejected non-empty add, and `restore_to(pre_add_head)` plus `despawn_brep_entity` cleanup where stored tracked id remains but liveness/render-mesh fields are false.
Run `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib cad_scene_inspection --no-fail-fast`, `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib add_cad_cuboid_restore_to_pre_add_head_cleans_projection_state --no-fail-fast`, `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib`, `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`, and `git diff --check`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required):** After the implementation is complete and verified, follow the Self-rearm protocol in this file's header: append exactly one bounded next-task-source AUDIT task as task 155 - a source/docs-read-only audit of the post-tracked-CAD-liveness inspection boundary - or, if no bounded in-policy next task exists, append a single `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` line instead. The task 155 audit must require dispatcher-provided GitHub-state snapshot evidence for queue/already-filed-task claims, must compare current source before choosing the next feature, and must itself carry this Self-rearm final-step requirement. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

body:
Add a read-only `EditorShell::cad_scene_inspection()`-style API and snapshot type for CAD graph/projection/renderability after `add_cad_cuboid_to_empty_scene`; keep it headless and read-only.
MAY edit `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/{commands.rs,mod.rs,tests.rs}` and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`; MUST NOT touch `render_path.rs`, `editor-actions`, `cad-core`, `cad-projection`, UI/host crates, Cargo, workflows, automation, render/GPU behavior, CommandBus, save/load, camera, or viewport behavior.
Done: snapshot distinguishes fresh shell, successful first cuboid add, rejected non-empty add, and restore/despawn cleanup; tests assert one CAD node/root, one `BRepHandle`, one `render_mesh_for` mesh, no prebuilt substitution, and no stale entity/mesh after cleanup.
Execution note: uploaded mesh count is a PURE read of `EditorShell.meshes` / `self.meshes.len()`; do NOT initialize render state, upload meshes, resume the window, or touch `render_path.rs`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required):** After the implementation is complete and verified, follow the Self-re-arm protocol in this file's header: append exactly one bounded next-task-source AUDIT task as task 153 - a source/docs-read-only audit of the post-inspection CAD/editor-shell feature boundary - or, if no bounded in-policy next task exists, append a single `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` line instead. title: Add one CAD cuboid primitive to an empty CAD scene

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Add a bounded shell entry point that inserts exactly one CuboidOp into an empty/new CAD scene using existing public APIs: begin_operation -> add_operator -> set_root -> commit -> projection tick/spawn if needed.
MAY edit editor-shell lifecycle commands/mod/tests plus render_path only for a minimal existing-path refresh hook; MUST NOT edit editor-actions/CommandBus, cad-core, cad-projection, UI/host, Cargo, workflows, or automation.
Done: the cuboid renders and is frameable/selectable through existing render_mesh_for / selected-CAD-bounds paths, with focused add + restore_to(pre_add_head) projection-cleanup tests and no global undo claim.
Self-re-arm (final step, required): after the implementation is complete and verified, follow the Self-re-arm protocol in this file's header: append exactly one bounded next-task-source AUDIT task as task 151 (mirror the audit STRUCTURE, not any "no successor" rule), or, if no bounded in-policy next task exists, append a `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` line. Copy this Self-re-arm requirement into the audit task you author. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

title: AI dispatch NEEDS_HUMAN: CAD/CommandBus first-mutation recommendation (task 149)

labels.name: needs-human
labels.description: AI dispatch paused for human review/decision

body:
The autonomous dispatch loop's task 149 (ISSUE-403, read-only CAD/CommandBus preflight) recorded a NEEDS_HUMAN recommendation in `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` and paused. Per the gate, it did NOT append an implementation task. Approve, redirect, or hold before any CAD mutation code is authored.

**Recommendation (Codex preflight):**
- **Proposed mutation:** a bus-routed "add one CAD cuboid primitive" command as the first real CAD mutation — submit one reversible CAD action through `CommandBus` after approving a narrow editor-owned command context (the current `Action::apply/revert` contract only receives `&mut World`, while live CAD state lives in `EditorShell` as `cad_world`/`cad_graph`/`projection`/`cad_entity`).
- **Edit surface:** `crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs`, `bus.rs` (+ action tests); `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `mod.rs`; `render_path.rs`; focused tests. Reuse existing `CadGraph::begin_operation/graph_mut/commit`, `OperatorGraph::add_operator/set_root`, `CadProjection::spawn_brep_entity/tick`.
- **Risks:** widening the bus action context beyond World-only; keeping graph commit, projection tick/cache invalidation, render-mesh upload, undo/revert, dirty-state, save-mark, and save/load coherent on success AND failed apply/revert.
- **Verification:** action unit coverage for the widened context + bus undo/redo/dirty/save-mark; lifecycle tests proving add-cuboid submit/undo/redo round-trips graph/projection/world/render; save/load dirty tests; render/projection tests showing the primitive is frameable/selectable.
- **Why smallest:** adding one primitive needs only create/commit/project/display/undo/dirty/save-mark — fewer concerns than delete, transform, edit-params, clear/replace, or doc-lifecycle alternatives.

Filed manually after the driver's auto-filing failed twice; see notification follow-up.

title: Audit CAD/CommandBus first mutation boundary

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Perform a read-only CAD/CommandBus first-mutation preflight and recommend the single smallest coherent editor/CAD mutation boundary for human approval.
MAY edit only `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`, and generated dispatch artifacts; MUST NOT edit source, tests, Cargo, workflows, scripts, schemas, routing, plugin, clipboard, camera, CAD, projection, CommandBus, undo/dirty, or save/load behavior.
Answer Q1-Q5 covering current CommandBus contract/action context, CAD projection/open-save-load/undo/dirty/save-mark authority, possible first mutations, exact human decision needed, and the recommended smallest coherent mutation.
As final step, append exactly one `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - CAD/CommandBus first-mutation recommendation` marker plus the required five-part â€œRecommendation for human approvalâ€^] block; MUST NOT append task 150 or any implementation task.
The recommendation block must include: Proposed mutation, Exact edit surface, Risks, Verification, and Why smallest coherent boundary.

Use only the dispatcher GitHub-state snapshot embedded in the issue body for queue/already-filed evidence; do not use `gh`, browser, network, or live GitHub state.
Record the pre-edit heading check, status-doc cross-check, candidate-class greps, `git diff --name-only`, `git diff --check`, and `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize ... -DryRun`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required):** After the audit is complete and verified, follow the Self-re-arm protocol in this file's header: append exactly one bounded FEATURE task as task 149, carrying this same Self-re-arm final-step requirement so the chain continues, or, if no bounded in-policy next task exists, append a single `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` line instead. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this. The task 149 feature must include explicit `MAY edit`, `MUST NOT edit`, `Done criteria`, `Verification`, and `Halt conditions` sections.

title: Reset stale viewport double-click on reset camera

labels.name: ai-auto

body:
Implement `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` task 145 exactly: when `EditorShell::reset_camera` runs via View -> Reset Camera / Frame Scene or Home, clear pending `ViewportLeftDoubleClick` state before a later in-threshold left press can complete a stale double-click.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`, and generated dispatch artifacts; preserve all task-145 MUST-NOT surfaces.
Done: focused tests prove first viewport left press + View/Home reset-camera + second in-threshold left press does not frame stale scene/selected bounds, while existing reset-camera and viewport double-click behavior remain unchanged.
Verification: `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib reset_camera`; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib viewport_left_double_click`; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib viewport_mouse_wheel`; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib viewport_focus_loss`; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib viewport_cursor_left`; `rg -n "^143\.|^144\.|^145\.|^146\." .ai/dispatch.tasks.md`; `git diff --name-only`; `git diff --check`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required):** After the implementation is complete
   and verified, follow the Self-re-arm protocol in this file's header: append

   into the audit task you author. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this

title: Reset stale viewport double-click on focus loss

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement task 141: reset pending `ViewportLeftDoubleClick` state on `WindowEvent::Focused(false)` / `handle_window_focus_change(false)`, while preserving existing focus-loss drag cancellation/cursor-grab release and focus-gained no-op behavior.
Scope is `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, allowed status/task docs, and generated dispatch artifacts only.
Add focused lifecycle coverage for first left press -> focus loss -> second in-threshold left press not framing scene or selected CAD bounds; run the listed focused editor-shell tests plus `git diff --name-only` and `git diff --check`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required):** After the implementation is complete and verified, follow the Self-re-arm protocol in this file's header: append exactly one bounded next-task-source AUDIT task as task 142 â€” a "Post-focus-loss-double-click-reset Phase 9 next-task source audit" (docs/source-read-only) â€” or, if no bounded in-policy next task exists, append a `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> â€” <reason>` line instead. Mirror task 140's audit STRUCTURE (sections, scope discipline) but **NOT** its "no task N+1 is appended" / "rg ^142 returns no matches" criteria: task 142 is itself a self-re-arming audit, so its done-criteria MUST require appending exactly one bounded FEATURE task 143 (carrying the copied Self-re-arm requirement). Copy this Self-re-arm (final step) requirement into the audit task you author. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this (already in `MAY edit`).

title: Frame selected CAD entities on viewport double-click

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement task 139: on qualifying viewport left double-click, frame selected CAD projection entity bounds first, falling back to existing scene-wide `reset_camera()`.
In scope: `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, status docs/task bookkeeping, and this dispatch's artifacts.
Use only existing `EditorShell` state plus read-only render-mesh/AABB helpers such as `CadProjection::render_mesh_for`, `compute_aabb_union`, and `isometric_camera_for_bounds`.
Preserve the existing double-click detector, single-click face-pick path, View camera commands, wheel zoom, orbit, pan, viewport hit testing, and command routing.
Do not edit CAD/projection crates, editor-ui, editor-egui-host, editor-actions, Cargo files, workflows, automation, or add commands/accelerators.
Done: selected single/multiple CAD bounds frame correctly; no selection/unresolved/non-finite/prebuilt path falls back to frame-all; tests cover both plus unchanged face-pick behavior.

title: Cancel viewport drags when cursor leaves

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement cursor-leave handling in `editor-shell`: clear `cursor_pos`, reset the viewport double-click tracker, cancel active orbit/pan drags, and release `CursorGrabMode::None` only when a drag was active.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, status/task docs, and generated ISSUE artifacts.
MUST NOT change camera math, viewport hit testing, picking, wheel zoom, frame-all, command routing, shortcuts, plugin/runtime, OS clipboard, CAD/CommandBus, save/load, Cargo, workflows, or automation.
Add focused tests for active orbit cancel, active pan cancel, combined drag release-once, no-active no-release, cursor-position clearing, and stale double-click reset.
Verify with the taskâ€™s focused `rge-editor-shell` tests, `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib`, `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`, task-heading grep, `git diff --name-only`, and `git diff --check`.

title: Release viewport drag cursor grab on focus loss

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement task 135 from `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`: cancel active viewport orbit/pan drag state on `WindowEvent::Focused(false)` and release the existing cursor grab only when a viewport drag was active.
Scope is `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, and status/task docs.
Preserve current camera math, viewport hit testing, wheel zoom, face picking, frame-all, command routing, shortcuts, plugin, clipboard, CAD, undo/dirty, and save/load behavior.
Done when focused tests cover active orbit/pan focus-loss cancellation plus the no-active-drag no-release case, and the listed editor-shell checks/fmt/diff gates pass.

title: Make conflicted shortcuts non-executable

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement task 123: keyboard execution must not fire a shortcut while `ResolveResult::conflicts` contains that shortcut.
Scope to editor-ui menu/shortcut resolution and focused tests, with only minimal editor-shell accelerator parity/comments if needed plus status/task docs.
Keep `command_for_shortcut` / `AcceleratorTable::resolve` first-winner behavior for display and diagnostics.
Preserve hidden/predicate-filtered entries releasing slots and disabled-visible entries remaining non-executable.
Do not change `MenuCommandHandoff`, `route_menu_command`, plugin runtime, OS/typed clipboard, CAD/CommandBus, camera, Cargo, workflows, or automation.
Done when tests cover conflicted suppression, unconflicted execution, disabled suppression, predicate filtering, diagnostics order, and no task 124 is appended.

title: fix(dispatch): guard hardening — path anchoring + assess stdin delivery + diagnosability
body:
## What & why

Hardens the guard before any further autonomous ticks — fixes the two
safety-layer bugs the first live guarded run (TICK114B/C) surfaced, per the
operator's sequence: **patch guard safety first → then merge #365 → then one
guarded tick for task 115.**

## 1. Path anchoring (TICK114B died at startup)

PowerShell cmdlets resolve relative paths against `$PWD`; `[System.IO.File]`
APIs resolve against the **process .NET current directory**. With a relative
`-WatchRoot` and mismatched anchors, the guard created its watch dir in one
place and aimed its first log write at another — dying before `START`.

Fix: absolutize `-WatchRoot` against `$PWD` (join **before** `GetFullPath`, so
normalization never consults the .NET cwd) + pin
`[System.Environment]::CurrentDirectory` to `$PWD`.

## 2. Assess delivery (TICK114C's "no activity" verdicts)

`& claude -p $rubric` passed the multi-line rubric as one argv. Under PS 5.1 the
JSON format spec's **embedded quotes shatter the argument** during native argv
quoting + npm-shim re-splat — the "Recent activity" tail never reached the
model. **Reproduced through the guard's exact code path**: a blatant `rm -rf` +
skipped-gate anomaly → `verdict=ok "no recent activity was provided"`.

Fix: prompt delivered via **stdin** through a new mockable seam
(`Invoke-MonitorModel`). Same anomaly now →
`verdict=abort "Executor ran a destructive 'rm -rf' … and skipped the VERIFY
gate while publishing anyway"`.

## 3. Diagnosability (operator requirement)

Every assessment persists its exact prompt (`assess-NNN.prompt.txt` in the watch
dir) and logs `ASSESSCTX … promptChars=N activityChars=M`, so any future "no
activity" claim is immediately attributable to construction vs delivery.

## Validation

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| New Pester: TICK114B recreation (child with `$PWD` ≠ .NET cwd + relative `-WatchRoot`) | completes, log under `$PWD`, nothing under the stale cwd |
| New Pester: assess seam (Mock) — canary in prompt + persisted, abort propagates, empty fails closed | green |
| Full `GuardSafetyMonitor` suite (incl. live final-drain + multi-tick) | **64/64** |
| PSScriptAnalyzer (repo settings) | 0 findings |
| Dry-run complete / hardrule / llm-abort | 0 / 2 / 2 |
| Real-claude smoke through the patched path (local) | anomaly → **abort**, prompt persisted with activity |

Two files: `Invoke-AiDispatchGuard.ps1`, `tools/dispatch-tests/GuardSafetyMonitor.Tests.ps1`.
No Rust; no behavior change to driver/queue/loop. Held for human merge.

title: docs: external-engine lessons ledger (five-pressure harvest practice)
body:
## What & why

`docs/EXTERNAL_ENGINE_LESSONS.md` — systematizes harvesting patterns from
comparable engines into RGE planning, the way kariyer/WTF do with their
"automation for ideas" backlog, **adapted to RGE's architecture freeze.**

The crux: kariyer promotes ideas when a *phase opens* (forecast). RGE is under
`PLAN` §0.6 (post-v0.8 subsystems need ≥3 concrete failure scenarios, not
forecast). So this ledger keeps kariyer's *mechanism* (capture backlog,
reporter-not-gate, docs churn only on promotion) but sets the **promotion bar to
RGE's pressure gate** — and splits the two bars across a **two-gate pipeline** so
we get both the faster harvest *and* the strict commit:

- **Gate A (phase/roadmap)** → *prepare*: mine donors + draft a design sketch in
  the ledger (`staged`). Commits nothing, freely discardable → forecast-OK.
- **Gate B (§0.6 pressure)** → *promote*: only real pressure graduates a row to
  ADR/`PLAN`/build. The phase trigger can never reach this alone.

## Organizing axis — the five kernel pressures

From the OpenClaw↔RGE kernel comparison: **composition · declarative content ·
subsystem boundaries · execution-time · authority-arbitration**. Each harvested
pattern is tagged by pressure + RGE locus + promotion gate + **risk if
misapplied**.

## Seeded rows (6, from OpenClaw)

- LES-001 cooperative `Process` chains → `kernel/job-system` (both gates)
- LES-002 ms-budgeted event drain → `kernel/events` (pressure-only)
- LES-003 LRU + refcount reclaim → `kernel/asset-streaming` stub (both gates)
- LES-004 real archetypes → `kernel/ecs` full-scan (pressure-only)
- **LES-005** physics façade → **n-a** (recorded divergence: RGE does
  physics-as-plugin, not a façade)
- **LES-006** two-model truth ownership → **no donor** (authority-arbitration is
  where RGE cannot borrow blindly — its stated #1 risk)

## Guardrails (keeps it doctrine-consistent)

One markdown file. No new lint, no ADR-for-the-process, no parallel authority.
Staging commits nothing; anti-sunk-cost is explicit; no prototypes on forecast;
deliberate divergences are recorded. A reporter script is deferred until the
ledger earns one.

Docs-only — the Rust gate is unaffected (covered by CI on clean runners). Held
for human merge.

title: feat(dispatch): flight-ready guard — live watch path + hardened source-tagged hard rules
body:
## What & why

"Make flight-ready first" — this makes the Claude watch/safety role **operational
for a live run** (not just a dry-run scaffold) and applies the fixes from the
Codex control review of #316. After this lands, the only thing between "built" and
"flying" is the explicit arming step.

## 1. Live watch path (`Invoke-GuardLiveRun` — previously threw `not armed`)

The guard now supervises a real autonomous run:
- launches the driver (`-DriverCommand`, default `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`) as a
  `Start-Process` child, redirecting stdout/stderr and tailing them incrementally
  into `watch.log` + `events.jsonl`;
- **classifies each line by source** (`signal` / `command` / `prose`), tracks
  liveness by output growth + process state;
- **assesses** every `-AssessIntervalSec` via `claude -p` (fail-safe: an
  unparseable/invalid verdict ⇒ **abort**); `-MockAssess` substitutes a scripted
  verdict for offline smoke tests;
- on any abort **kills the child process tree** (`taskkill /T /F`) + writes
  `abort-report.md`; exit `0` = completed, `2` = aborted.

## 2. Hard-rule fixes (Codex #316 review)

| # | Finding | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 1/2 | protected-ref / force-push regexes too narrow | broadened: `origin main`, `HEAD:main`, `refs/heads/main`, `+main`, URL remote; `--force` / `-f` / `+refspec` |
| 3 | hard rules scanned raw prose → false positives | **source-tagged matching** — command patterns match only `command`-classified text, signal patterns only `signal` text; a TASK/rubric that *mentions* a dangerous command can never trip a rule |
| 4 | `AUTONOMOUS_WATCH.md` overstated live capability | live behaviors documented as **built** (the live path is now real) |
| 5 | doc claimed `-DryRun` is the default | removed — it's a `[switch]`; omitting it enters the live path |

## Two bugs the live smoke test caught (and fixed)

- `$proc.ExitCode` returned `null` → a clean run falsely aborted. Fixed by caching
  `$proc.Handle`.
- `taskkill` on an already-exited child trapped under the script's `EAP=Stop` (PS
  5.1 native-stderr trap) → crashed the guard mid-abort. EAP-isolated (same fix
  applied to the `claude` call).

title: feat(dispatch): Claude active safety-guard scaffold for autonomous Codex dispatch (dry-run only)
body:
## What & why

Owner-directed reconfiguration of the dispatch automation's role + safety model:

- **Codex = the decision + execution layer** ("the human"): plans, selects the
  next task, **executes**, control-reviews, and **auto-publishes** on its own
  `pass` verdict.
- **Claude = watch-only**: leaves the executor seat and becomes a dedicated
  **safety-monitor** that records everything, assesses for anomalies, and
  **terminates the run with a report** if something is wrong.

This **reverses the previous human-only merge gate**. The replacement safety net
is three-layered: (1) the §18 *delegated-human auto-publish* bounds in
`AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`; (2) Codex's `pass` control verdict; (3) the new
**Claude watch veto** in this PR.

**Build + dry-run only — nothing is armed.** The scheduler is not registered,
`-PublishMode main` is not invoked, and there is no live run against RGE.

## Changes (additive — no existing script modified)

- **`Invoke-AiDispatchGuard.ps1`** — the Claude active safety-guard. The
  action-taking sibling of the existing passive `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1` dashboard
  (which keeps its read-only, non-participating contract). It:
  - records each phase to `.ai/dispatch-watch/<id>/watch.log` + `events.jsonl`
    (timestamped, sequence-numbered, structured JSONL);
  - assesses via **deterministic hard rules** (protected-branch push, force-push,
    `VERIFY FAILED`, non-zero gate exit, `BLOCKED`/`FAILED`/`NEEDS_HUMAN` verdict,
    max-runtime, max-correction-rounds) **plus a Claude LLM verdict**
    (fail-safe: an unparseable/invalid monitor response is treated as a halt);
  - on abort, kills the child process **tree** (`taskkill /T /F`) and writes
    `abort-report.md` (trigger, reason, activity tail, follow-up).
  - `-DryRun` drives a **hermetic** scripted sequence (no real loop, no Claude
    call, no publish) with three outcomes: `complete`, `hardrule`, `llm-abort`.
- **`AUTONOMOUS_WATCH.md`** — design + safety posture; documents what is reused
  vs. built vs. **specced** (the additive `-Executor codex` loop swap — deferred
  because it cannot be smoke-tested under "dry-run only" without arming the real
  Codex-executor path) vs. **explicitly not done**.

## Validation

| Gate | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Dry-run `complete` | exit 0, `disposition=completed`, no anomaly |
| Dry-run `hardrule` | exit 2, aborts at `VERIFY FAILED`, report written |
| Dry-run `llm-abort` | exit 2, aborts at first assess (`llm-veto`), report written |
| PSScriptAnalyzer (repo `PSScriptAnalyzerSettings.psd1`, Error+Warning) | 0 findings on new script |
| Pester `tools/dispatch-tests` | 308/308 |
| Rust verify gate (`.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`) | unaffected (no Rust change) |

## Not in this PR (explicit follow-ups, each a separate operator decision)

- The `-Executor codex` loop swap (specced precisely in `AUTONOMOUS_WATCH.md`).
- Wiring the guard's live path (`Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` as a supervised child).
- Registering the scheduler / any live autonomous run.

title: MENU-ENABLEMENT-DOC-RECONCILE: record #313/#314 (dynamic menu enablement shipped), docs-only
body:
**MENU-ENABLEMENT-DOC-RECONCILE — forward-only state-doc reconcile for #313 + #314.** Registry-driven dynamic menu enablement landed, but the live state docs still listed "the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve" as *deferred* and framed the bespoke `MenuStateHandoff`/`play_item_enabled` greying as the live mechanism. **Docs-only, 4 files, forward-only** (mirrors W08-ACCELERATOR-DOC-RECONCILE #312).

## What it records
#313/#314 shipped **dynamic menu enablement**:
- **#313 (`459c689`, MENU-ENABLED-SUBSTRATE)** — editor-ui "disabled-but-visible" substrate: a separate `MenuEntry.enabled` predicate (vs the filtering visibility `predicate`), `ResolvedEntry.enabled` computed without filtering, `enabled_command_for_shortcut` (binding `command_for_shortcut` unchanged → W08.3 parity guard intact), `PredicateContext` `can_*`/`is_editing`; `default_editor_menu` gates File on `is_editing` + Play on `can_*`. Additive, behaviour-neutral.
- **#314 (`10790ba`, MENU-DYNAMIC-RESOLVE)** — host caches the `MenuRegistry` and **re-resolves per frame** against the live `PredicateContext` (greys via `ResolvedEntry.enabled`); shell publishes the live context through a new `Handoff<PredicateContext>` + routes the keyboard via `enabled_command_for_shortcut`; the bespoke `MenuStateHandoff`/`MenuStateSnapshot` (deleted)/`play_item_enabled` channel is **retired**.

It **supersedes** the W08-ACCELERATOR (#312) snapshot's "dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve … deferred (menu resolves once with `PredicateContext::default()`; static index)" line and the PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC (#302) bespoke-greying framing. **Rough-edge fix:** File Save/Open/Save-As grey out outside Editing (were always-enabled → looked clickable but no-op'd during Play).

## Deferred list — narrowed carefully (NOT claimed done)
Play/View accelerator display+execution; `AcceleratorTable` conflict UI/population; dynamic toggle labels (Play⇄Pause, camera-state View); plugin menu entries; **host→shell FIFO menu-click replacement** (only the *keyboard's* enablement was unified onto the registry path, not the click transport); generalized command execution beyond the canonical entries; the **visibility predicates** (filtering `predicate`) remain available but unused by `default_editor_menu`.

title: MENU-DYNAMIC-RESOLVE: per-frame menu re-resolve + live PredicateContext; retire bespoke greying
body:
**MENU-DYNAMIC-RESOLVE — per-frame menu re-resolve + live `PredicateContext`; retire the bespoke greying.** Second of two carves for the dynamic-menu-enablement design (D1 = MENU-ENABLED-SUBSTRATE #313 landed the editor-ui substrate). Wires the host + shell to the canonical registry enablement path and **retires** the bespoke `MenuStateHandoff` / `MenuStateSnapshot` / `play_item_enabled` channel — one enablement path now, and the File "looks-clickable-but-no-ops-during-Play" rough edge is fixed. 10 files, −79 net.

## What
**editor-egui-host**
- `handoff.rs` — `MenuStateHandoff = Handoff<MenuStateSnapshot>` → `PredicateContextHandoff = Handoff<PredicateContext>` (aliased here, parameterized by editor-ui's `PredicateContext` over editor-state's generic `Handoff<T>` — no `editor-state → editor-ui` edge).
- `menu.rs` — `build_main_menu_entries()` → `project_main_menu(&MenuRegistry, &PredicateContext)` returning `(label, accel, command, enabled)` 4-tuples; **delete** `play_item_enabled`.
- `lib.rs` — cache the `MenuRegistry` (built once); `render` **re-resolves it each frame** against the live `PredicateContext` (acquired from the handoff) and greys each item via the resolved `enabled` → `add_enabled`. The 4 cached entry-vec fields + the `menu_state` acquire are gone.

**editor-shell**
- `lifecycle/mod.rs` — `menu_state_snapshot()` → `predicate_context()` (fills `play_state` / `can_*` / `is_editing` / `has_selection` from the live `PlayState` + selection); the keyboard `window_event` path resolves against the live context via **`enabled_command_for_shortcut`**, so a disabled accelerator (Ctrl+S while greyed) does not fire.
- `render_path.rs` — the per-frame publish + the init clone-in switch to the predicate-context handoff.

**editor-state**
- **delete** `menu_state_snapshot.rs` (the bespoke `MenuStateSnapshot`); keep the generic `Handoff<T>`.

## Behaviour
File Save/Open/Save-As **and** the Play items now grey out per the live state via the **one** registry path (the parallel mechanism is gone). `command_for_shortcut` + the W08 parity guard are unchanged. Menu **clicks** still flow through the `MenuCommandHandoff` FIFO — only the *keyboard's* enablement was unified onto `enabled_command_for_shortcut`, not the click transport.

## Tests
- editor-shell `menu_state_snapshot_tracks_play_state` → `predicate_context_tracks_play_state`.
- host `play_item_enabled_*` retired → `enablement_tracks_context` (asserts resolved `enabled` per editing/playing context; **disabled File items stay present, not hidden**).
- the label/command/order + accelerator-display tests migrate onto `project_main_menu` via a 3-tuple `menu_entries()` helper (assertions unchanged).

## Verification
- `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean; `cargo check -p rge-editor-state -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --all-targets` clean (one pre-existing `TopBottomPanel` deprecation, unrelated).
- `cargo test` on the three crates — all pass (editor-shell lib 226 + integration, host menu tests, editor-state).
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → `VERIFY OK 7/7` + 10/10 architecture lints.

title: MENU-ENABLED-SUBSTRATE: registry disabled-but-visible enablement (editor-ui, additive)
body:
**MENU-ENABLED-SUBSTRATE — registry disabled-but-visible enablement (editor-ui only, additive).** First of **two** carves for the owner-approved "dynamic menu enablement" design.

## Why
The menu registry's `predicate` is **visibility-only** — a false predicate FILTERS the entry out of the menu *and* the accelerator index. There's no "greyed-but-present" concept; greying is a separate bespoke `MenuStateHandoff`/`play_item_enabled` path used only for Play items, while PIE-gated **File** items (Save/Open/Save-As) look clickable but silently no-op during Play (the audit's MEDIUM rough edge). This dispatch adds the canonical *enablement* concept in editor-ui; the follow-up (**MENU-DYNAMIC-RESOLVE**) wires the host/shell to it + **retires** the bespoke channel.

**This PR is additive and behaviour-neutral** — no consumer reads `enabled` yet (the host still projects the 3-tuple; the shell keyboard still uses `command_for_shortcut`). The consumers compile unchanged.

## What
- **`predicate.rs`** — extend `PredicateContext` (`#[non_exhaustive]`, additive) with `can_play`/`can_pause`/`can_stop`/`can_step` (Play-item enablement) + `is_editing` (File gate).
- **`entry.rs`** — add a separate `enabled: Predicate` field to `MenuEntry` (default always-enabled) + `with_enabled` builder; clarify `predicate` = VISIBILITY (removes the entry) vs `enabled` = ENABLEMENT (greyed but present, accelerator intact).
- **`registry.rs`** — `ResolvedEntry.enabled: bool`, computed in `resolve_slot` (NOT the Step-1 visibility filter, so disabled entries STAY in `by_point`); `shortcut_commands` now stores `(Command, enabled)`; `command_for_shortcut` keeps its binding semantics (W08 parity guard intact); **new** `enabled_command_for_shortcut` returns the command only when enabled — the resolver the keyboard execution path (D2) uses so a greyed accelerator doesn't fire.
- **`default_menu.rs`** — attach enablement predicates: File → `is_editing`, Play → the matching `can_*`; Edit/View stay always-enabled.

## Tests
A disabled-but-visible entry stays present with `enabled == false` while `command_for_shortcut` still returns its binding and `enabled_command_for_shortcut` returns `None`; `default_editor_menu` enablement tracks editing vs playing contexts (File greyed while playing, Ctrl+S still bound for display but withheld from execution); the new context booleans default `false`.

## Not touched (per scope — that's D2)
editor-egui-host / editor-shell / editor-state are untouched. The per-frame re-resolve, the `Handoff<PredicateContext>`, the keyboard switch to `enabled_command_for_shortcut`, and the retirement of `MenuStateHandoff`/`MenuStateSnapshot`/`play_item_enabled` are the **MENU-DYNAMIC-RESOLVE** follow-up.

## Verification
- `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean; `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui --all-targets` clean; `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor-egui-host` clean (consumers compile — `command_for_shortcut` unchanged).
- `cargo test -p rge-editor-ui` — all menu tests pass (incl. the new enablement tests).
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → `VERIFY OK 7/7` + 10/10 architecture lints.

Reviewer: OpenAI Codex (execute-then-review). Merge gated on explicit human authorization.

title: W08-4-RETIRE-SHADOW: retire shadowed EditorKeyCommand File/Edit binds + re-anchor parity guard
body:
**W08.4 — retire the shadowed `EditorKeyCommand` File/Edit binds; re-anchor the parity guard.** Final carve of the accelerator-execution thread (W08.1 #308 → W08.2 #309 → W08.3 #310 → this). After the W08.3 cutover, `EditorKeyCommand::{Undo, Redo, Save, SaveAsProject}` were **shadows** — the keyboard no longer reached them (the menu path caught Ctrl+Z/Y/S/Shift+S first), and their keystroke→command literals were **duplicated** between `from_key_press` (editor-shell) and `default_editor_menu` (editor-ui). This removes the duplication: the File/Edit accelerators now live **only** in the canonical menu. **Pure de-duplication — no runtime behaviour change** (the menu path already does exactly what the retired variants did; W08.3 proved equivalence). 9 files, −139 net lines.

## What
- **`commands.rs`** — remove the `Undo`/`Redo`/`Save`/`SaveAsProject` variants + their `from_key_press` arms (drop the `Ctrl+Shift` block) + their `handle_key_command` arms. `from_key_press` now returns `Some` only for `Ctrl+2/0/4`; `handle_key_command` dispatches only the three time-scale binds. (`EditorKeyCommand` is **kept**, not renamed — the time-scale binds have no menu home and aren't duplicated. `BusError` import retained — still used by `submit_action`/`undo_command`/`redo_command`.)
- **Parity guard re-anchor (`accelerator.rs`)** — `keyboard_map_and_menu_agree_on_shared_accelerators` now drives all **five** File/Edit binds (Ctrl+O/S/Shift+S/Z/Y) via `keycode_to_shortcut → command_for_shortcut` and asserts (a) the menu binds each to its `Command` and (b) `from_key_press` returns `None` for each (the retirement holds — no shadow can drift). The time-scale block is unchanged. `from_key_press` is now the time-scale executor, no longer a shared-bind anchor.

## Test migration (the retirement breaks every retired-variant site — a full consumer census, not a sample)
- **`keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`** — deleted 4 `EditorKeyCommand`-driven behaviour tests (exact duplicates of the W08.3 `route_menu_command_*` tests), migrated 3 unique-coverage tests (redo-empty / undo-after-all-undone / three-round-trips) to `route_menu_command`, updated the 2 `from_key_press` guards (Ctrl+Z/Y/S + Ctrl+Shift+S now → `None`), refreshed the header.
- **`time_scale_test.rs`** — migrated 6 Undo/Redo sites to `route_menu_command` (the `SetTimeScale*` sites stay); +file-level `Command` import; dropped 2 now-unused local imports.
- **`inspector_snapshot_smoke.rs`** — migrated the Undo/Redo round-trip to `route_menu_command`.
- **`lifecycle/tests.rs`** — migrated the Ctrl+S Save test to `route_menu_command`; repurposed the Ctrl+Shift+S decode test to pin `from_key_press → None` for the retired binds.

> Note: `inspector_snapshot_smoke.rs`, `lifecycle/tests.rs`, `save_request.rs`, and `editor-egui-host/lib.rs` were **not** in the initial scope estimate — a comprehensive `EditorKeyCommand`/`handle_key_command` census surfaced them (the first grep was truncated). All are fixed here; shipping without them would have been compile errors + broken doc links.

## Stale-prose / broken-link sweep
- **editor-ui `default_menu.rs`** — inverted the header: the menu is the **canonical** source now (was "mirrors `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press`").
- **editor-shell `save_request.rs`** — rewrote the now-broken intra-doc links `[Save](EditorKeyCommand::Save)` / `[SaveAsProject](…)` to the `route_menu_command` path.
- **editor-egui-host `lib.rs`** — updated the egui-consumed example handler reference.

## Not touched
No `Cargo.toml` / crate-edge change; no `default_editor_menu` definition change; no rename of `EditorKeyCommand` / `handle_key_command` (deferred). Runtime behaviour of time-scale / playback / R / Open/Save/Undo/Redo is unchanged.

## Verification
- `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean; `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host --all-targets` clean (no unused-import / dead-code; one pre-existing `TopBottomPanel` deprecation, unrelated).
- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor-ui` — 226 lib + all integration suites pass (keyboard round-trip 14, time-scale 22, inspector 11, accelerator 5, …).
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → `VERIFY OK 7/7` + 10/10 architecture lints.

title: W08-3-KEYSTROKE-CUTOVER: route un-consumed keystrokes through the canonical menu
body:
**W08.3 — route un-consumed keystrokes through the canonical menu.** Third carve of the accelerator-execution thread, on top of W08.1 (canonical menu source, #308) and W08.2 (`keycode_to_shortcut` bridge + parity guard, #309). The **first W08 step with a real behaviour change**: the live editor-shell keyboard path now consults the canonical menu (`default_editor_menu`) instead of a hardcoded `EditorKeyCommand` table + an inline Ctrl+O arm. The W08.2 parity guard is the safety net. 6 files, all in editor-shell.

## What
- **`render_path.rs` — extract the shared command sink.** Lift the per-`Command` dispatch out of `drain_and_route_menu_commands` into `pub fn route_menu_command(&mut self, command: Command)`. The `match` body is **verbatim** (same arms, same handlers, same `tracing` targets), so the **menu path is byte-identical**; the drain is now a thin loop calling it. `pub` mirrors `handle_key_command`'s "public so headless tests can drive it" precedent.
- **`lifecycle/mod.rs` — rewire the `window_event` keyboard ladder.** On each un-consumed `KeyDown`: resolve via `keycode_to_shortcut` → the resolved menu's `command_for_shortcut`, and if bound, route the **same `Command` the menu bar routes** through `route_menu_command`. This collapses the former `EditorKeyCommand` Save/SaveAs/Undo/Redo arm and the **inline Ctrl+O arm** into one menu-sourced path. `EditorKeyCommand` stays as the fallback for the execution-only **time-scale binds** (Ctrl+2/0/4, no menu home); **playback (Space/Escape) and plain-R are unchanged**. The menu is resolved on-demand — the shortcut→command index is static (every `default_editor_menu` entry is unconditional) and keydowns are human-frequency.

## Behaviour-equivalence (verified — no regression)
`handle_key_command`'s Save/SaveAs/Undo/Redo arms call the **same handlers** as the menu router's `Command::Save/SaveAs/Undo/Redo` (Undo/Redo even share the identical `NothingToUndo`/`NothingToRedo` swallow); Ctrl+O inline == menu `OpenFile`. Time-scale, playback, R untouched.

- **Sole diagnostic delta:** the Undo/Redo non-`NothingTo…` error `tracing::warn!` target moves from `…::lifecycle` to `…::menu` (the extracted body keeps the menu target). The swallowed-noop common case is unchanged; this only differs on a genuine bus error, which these never produce. No test asserts on tracing targets.
- **One intentional refinement:** the old inline arm `key == KeyO && ctrl` ignored Shift, so **Ctrl+Shift+O** also triggered Open. The canonical menu binds Ctrl+O precisely (CTRL only), so **Ctrl+Shift+O is now a no-op** — the ad-hoc-keybind → declared-accelerator correction the W08 thread exists for. Pinned by a test.

## Tests
- `accelerator.rs`: `menu_path_makes_ctrl_o_precise_about_shift` — Ctrl+O → `OpenFile`, Ctrl+Shift+O → `None`.
- `tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`: 4 new `route_menu_command` parity tests (Undo reverts / Redo re-applies / empty-stack no-op / Save writes + clears dirty) — proving the shared sink is behaviour-identical to the `handle_key_command` path, reusing the existing mock dialog/hook + seed helpers.

## Stale-prose sweep (un-deferring a capability)
`accelerator.rs` doc + comments (`from_key_press` is now the parity **anchor** + the time-scale executor, not the live executor for the shared binds); `open_request.rs` + `asset_reload.rs` headers (Ctrl+O is no longer a distinct inline "keyboard axis" — it routes via the menu path). The dated 2026-05-21 playback changelog entry is preserved (its EditorKeyCommand→playback fall-through is intact).

## Not touched (per scope)

title: W08-2-KEYCODE-PARITY: shell-local KeyCode->Shortcut translation + keyboard/menu parity guard
body:
**W08.2 — shell-local KeyCode→Shortcut translation + keyboard/menu parity guard.** Second carve of the accelerator-execution thread, on top of the W08.1 canonical menu source (#308). Lands the shell-local translation and a parity guard that locks the editor-shell keyboard map to the canonical menu — *before* any live input rewiring. **Zero behaviour change; the live keyboard path is untouched.** 2 files.

## Why
W08.1 made `editor-ui` the single source of truth (`default_editor_menu` + `command_for_shortcut`). The remaining risk is that editor-shell's executable keyboard map (`EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press`) and the menu definition silently diverge. W08.2 closes that with a test that makes divergence a hard failure — the payoff that justifies the whole thread — and provides the translation W08.3's cutover will route through.

## What
- **NEW `lifecycle/accelerator.rs` — `keycode_to_shortcut(KeyCode, ctrl, shift) -> Option<Shortcut>`:** the `rge_input::KeyCode` + Ctrl/Shift → `rge_editor_ui::menus::Shortcut` bridge. It lives in editor-shell because `editor-ui ↛ rge-input` (`forbidden-dep` rule 4) and editor-shell depends on both. Exhaustive over `KeyCode` (letters→`Key::Char` uppercase, digits→`Key::Digit`, F-keys→`Key::Function`, edit/nav/arrows→named `Key`s, the eight modifier keys→`None`). `pub` via `pub mod accelerator` (mirrors `EditorKeyCommand`), so the not-yet-wired fn is genuine public API rather than dead code.
- **Parity test `keyboard_map_and_menu_agree_on_shared_accelerators`:** drives both `from_key_press` and `default_editor_menu().command_for_shortcut` with the same `(KeyCode, ctrl, shift)` and asserts the same logical command for the four `EditorKeyCommand`-routed shared binds (Ctrl+S / Ctrl+Shift+S / Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y). Pins the two intentional asymmetries: **Ctrl+O** routes via the inline `window_event` arm (so `from_key_press → None`, menu → `OpenFile`); the **Ctrl+2/0/4** time-scale binds are execution-only (menu → `None`). A future menu entry for a time-scale key, or a changed keystroke literal on either side, fails the test.
- **`lifecycle/mod.rs`:** `pub mod accelerator;` + `pub use accelerator::keycode_to_shortcut;`.

## Not touched (per scope)
No live keyboard-path change (`window_event`'s arms + `from_key_press` are the executor, unchanged), no routing of the new translation into execution, no `EditorKeyCommand`/menu change, no `Cargo.toml`/crate-edge change. The live keystroke cutover (collapsing the `EditorKeyCommand` + inline Ctrl+O arms into `command_for_shortcut`) is **W08.3**.

## Verification
- `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean; `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --all-targets` clean (no dead-code warning on the new `pub fn`; one pre-existing `TopBottomPanel` deprecation in the host crate, unrelated).
- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell accelerator` — 4 tests (3 translation + the parity guard) pass.
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → `VERIFY OK 7/7` + 10/10 architecture lints.

title: W08-CANONICAL-MENU-SOURCE: move default editor menu into editor-ui + add Shortcut->Command resolver
body:
**W08.1 — canonical menu source.** First carve of the accelerator-execution thread from the read-only design pass. Establishes `editor-ui` as the single source of truth for the editor's four main menus and wakes the dormant `AcceleratorTable` for execution. **Zero behaviour change, no editor-shell touch.** 4 files.

## Why
The executable keyboard map (`editor-shell::EditorKeyCommand` + the inline `Ctrl+O` arm) and the display map (`editor-ui::MenuEntry.shortcut`, authored in the egui host) duplicate the keystroke literals, and `AcceleratorTable` is built by `resolve` but never queried (dormant). This dispatch creates the shared definition + the resolver both sides will route through, so the later cutover unifies them without a reverse crate edge (`editor-egui-host ↛ editor-shell`).

## What
- **editor-ui — NEW `menus/default_menu.rs`:** `pub default_editor_menu() -> MenuRegistry` (File/Edit/Play/View extension points + entries + File/Edit accelerators, moved verbatim from the host) + four `pub *_menu_point() -> ExtensionPoint` accessors. The single source of truth both consumers build from.
- **editor-ui — `ResolveResult::command_for_shortcut(&Shortcut) -> Option<&Command>`:** bridges the O(1) `AcceleratorTable` (Shortcut→EntryId) with `by_point` (EntryId→Command). Winner = first-registered (matches `AcceleratorTable::resolve`); unbound → `None`. The substrate editor-shell consumes in W08.2.
- **editor-egui-host — `build_main_menu_entries`:** now resolves `default_editor_menu()` and projects to the host's `(label, Option<display>, Command)` triples; the four local consts are gone. `play_item_enabled` / `menu_item` untouched.

## Behaviour-preserving
Display output is byte-identical — the **24 `menu_tests`** display-string + handoff-FIFO pins stay green. No editor-shell change, no `Cargo.toml`/edge change, no `Command`-variant change.

## Tests (editor-ui, new)
- The five shared accelerators (Ctrl+O / Ctrl+S / Ctrl+Shift+S / Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y) resolve to `OpenFile` / `Save` / `SaveAs` / `Undo` / `Redo` via `command_for_shortcut`.
- The canonical menu has zero conflicts and binds exactly five accelerators.
- Declaration order is File / Edit / Play / View; Play/View entries carry no accelerator.
- Resolver: winner-under-conflict + miss-returns-`None`.

## Deferred (not in this PR)
W08.2 = editor-shell `keycode_to_shortcut` translation + a parity test (`from_key_press` vs `command_for_shortcut` over the 5 shared binds); W08.3 = live keystroke cutover (route through `command_for_shortcut → Command → drain router`). No live input path is touched here.

## Verification
- `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean; `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host --all-targets` clean (one pre-existing `TopBottomPanel` deprecation, unrelated).
- editor-ui 35 menus tests + editor-egui-host 24 menu_tests pass.
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → `VERIFY OK 7/7` + 10/10 architecture lints.

`EditorShell::current_scene_bounds`' **CAD-projection arm** (`lifecycle/mod.rs ~1672-1678`) was untested — `reset_camera` (View ▸ Reset Camera, now user-reachable via the menu) could silently fall back to the default pose on the CAD authoring path with no test signal. The prebuilt-meshes arm and the `None` arm are already covered.

New `reset_camera_frames_cad_projection_scene` (`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`):
- Builds a **non-unit** (4×2×6) CAD cuboid via `CadGraph`/`CadProjection` — a 1×1×1 origin cube frames to the default (3,3,3) pose, which couldn't separate the CAD arm from the None-fallback.
- Computes the expected isometric framing from the **same** `projection.render_mesh_for(entity, &world)` call the branch makes, asserts it frames away from default, then constructs the shell via `with_world_projection_graph` (prebuilt empty + CAD fields set → the arm runs), clobbers the camera, calls `reset_camera()`, and asserts `editor_camera.target`/`.eye` reproduce the CAD framing. A regression that returns `None` (silent default fallback) now fails the test.

title: MENU-SHORTCUT-DISPLAY: show File/Edit accelerators next to menu items
body:
**Menu-deepening beat (display-only)** after #302/#303. File + Edit menu items now show their real keyboard accelerator next to the label, sourced from the existing `editor-ui::menus::Shortcut` substrate. **No command-execution change, no new crate edge, no keystroke-routing change** — clicking still dispatches; the accelerator is a passive hint.

| Menu item | Accelerator shown |
|---|---|
| File ▸ Open… | `Ctrl+O` |
| File ▸ Save | `Ctrl+S` |
| File ▸ Save As New Project… | `Ctrl+Shift+S` |
| Edit ▸ Undo | `Ctrl+Z` |
| Edit ▸ Redo | `Ctrl+Y` |

## How
- `build_main_menu_entries` attaches `.with_shortcut(Shortcut::new(..))` to the 3 File + 2 Edit entries; the projection widens `(label, Command)` → `(label, Option<accelerator-display>, Command)`, sourced straight from each resolved `MenuEntry.shortcut` via `Shortcut::display`. Play/View project `None`.
- `render`: a small `menu_item` helper builds each `egui::Button` + optional `shortcut_text` (egui 0.34 right-aligned weak text); the four menu loops call it. Play still passes its per-item PIE enablement (`play_item_enabled`).
- `menu_tests`: the four equality tests now pin the accelerator strings **and** Play/View=`None` (the deferral); round-trip destructures widened; one focused test asserts the File/Edit display strings + the deferral.

## Why File/Edit only (Play/View deferred)
Every accelerator shown is **real + executing**, mirroring the live editor-shell keystroke routing (`EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` for Ctrl+Z/Y/S/Shift+S, plus the `Ctrl+O` `handle_open_request` arm). Play's only "shortcut data" is the play-toolbar's **advisory** `F5/Shift+F5/Esc/F10` hints — which its own comment marks advisory and which don't match the **real** Space/Escape PIE binds. Surfacing Play would either show a non-executing hint or expose that advisory-vs-real mismatch — exactly the accelerator/execution-conflict this display-only dispatch avoids. View ▸ Reset Camera has no keystroke binding. `MenuEntry.shortcut` is the substrate's designated accelerator home (both editor-shell sites point at "editor-ui/menus / W08"); the deferred W08 execution work unifies the two by routing keystrokes through the resolved `AcceleratorTable`.

## Authority (no parallel authority)
The host **cannot** import editor-shell (reverse crate edge), so the accelerator *values* are authored to mirror the live bindings and a host test pins every display string. Today this is display-only; W08 makes it the single source.

## §1.3 line-cap note
The File/Edit additions pushed `editor-egui-host/lib.rs` back over the 1000-line Rule-3 cap (which #301 cleared via test extraction). Re-added the split-cap exemption annotation and **deferred the menu-module extraction to a dedicated follow-up (EGUIHOST-MENU-EXTRACTION)** — not bundled into this display-only PR, mirroring how #301 was itself a standalone extraction. Corrected the one now-stale `menu_tests.rs` cap claim.

## Verification

title: PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-ENABLE: grey out invalid Play-menu items per live PlayState
body:
**Feature — deepen the menu bar (beat 1).** The Play menu items (Play / Pause / Stop / Step) now **grey out** (disabled-but-visible) when the current `PlayState` makes the transition a no-op, instead of being always-clickable with a benign-swallowed no-op. First use of the W08 menu substrate beyond static breadth; establishes a live "menu state" channel from editor-shell to the host.

> **Correction (commit `bb1081f`, per Codex review):** adding `MenuStateHandoff` made it the *third* latest-only snapshot handoff, so the "two latest-only snapshot handoffs" / "Both are type aliases" / "the two handoffs above" / Inspector+SaveStatus-only listings went stale. A complete multiline sweep widened every live count/listing to three (+ added `MenuStateHandoff` to the listings) across `handoff.rs`, `lib.rs`, `mod.rs`, `render_path.rs`. The historical Rule-of-Three notes (the 3 *original* hand-written copies that motivated the shared generic) are preserved. Comments/docs only — logic byte-identical to `732b61f`. **Round 2 (`e722aab`):** one more `/`-separated listing (the `MenuCommandHandoff` doc) widened; an exhaustive separator-agnostic co-mention sweep now confirms every live Inspector+SaveStatus listing includes `MenuStateHandoff` (only the historical Rule-of-Three notes omit it, correctly). **Round 3 (`e762a76`):** the *parallel* SNAPSHOT-aggregator set (editor-state `lib.rs` + `handoff.rs` + editor-egui-host `lib.rs` dep-rationale) widened to include `MenuStateSnapshot`. **Round 4 (`cdc4879`):** the last live alias-over-`Handoff` listing (`editor-state/lib.rs:44`, a third locus, wrapped across lines) widened to include `MenuStateHandoff`. A definitive newline-crossing (`[\s\S]`) co-mention sweep across all crates now confirms every live listing includes the MenuState member; only the historical Rule-of-Three notes + the dated EDITOR-SAVE-STATUS-INDICATOR changelog correctly omit it.

## Authority discipline (the crux)
`PlayState` stays the **sole owner** of transition validity. editor-shell computes the 4 enablement booleans *from* `PlayState` and ships them; the host only routes booleans to buttons — **no validity rule is re-encoded** in editor-ui or the host.

## What lands
### `crates/editor-shell/src/play_state.rs`
- `PlayState::can_play / can_pause / can_stop / can_step` — derived from the canonical state (`can_play = !Playing`; `can_pause`/`can_stop` = `is_pie_active()`; `can_step` = `Paused`).
- **Consistency test** pinning each `can_*` to whether the real `play()`/`pause()`/`stop()`/`step()` returns `Ok` — so the enablement can't drift from the state machine.

### `crates/editor-state/src/menu_state_snapshot.rs` (NEW)
- `MenuStateSnapshot { play_can_start, play_can_pause, play_can_stop, play_can_step }` — a read-only **observation aggregator** (sibling to `SaveStatusSnapshot`, explicitly *not* a 6th coordination category, so `editor-state-ownership` Part A stays silent) + `all_enabled()` for the host's pre-first-publish fallback.

### `crates/editor-egui-host` (`handoff.rs` + `lib.rs`)
- `MenuStateHandoff = Handoff<MenuStateSnapshot>` (latest-only, mirrors `SaveStatusHandoff`); the host owns the `Arc` + exposes it. `render` acquires the snapshot (fallback `all_enabled()` before the first publish) and `ui.add_enabled(...)`s each Play item via a small `play_item_enabled` helper.

### `crates/editor-shell` (`lifecycle/mod.rs` + `render_path.rs`)
- `menu_state_handoff` field (cloned from the host in `init_render_state`) + `EditorShell::menu_state_snapshot()` (pure read from `PlayState::can_*`); published each frame beside `save_status`.

## Behaviour
- **Editing**: only Play enabled. **Playing**: Pause + Stop enabled. **Paused**: all four enabled. File / Edit / View items stay unconditionally enabled (this beat is Play-only).

## Tests
- `can_*`↔transition consistency (editor-shell); `menu_state_snapshot` tracks Editing/Playing/Paused (editor-shell); `play_item_enabled` 1:1 routing (editor-egui-host).

## Scope / verification
- **10 files, 0 `Cargo.toml`** (+378 / −34 at final head `cdc4879`; the growth over the initial +254/−4 is entirely the 4 stale-prose correction rounds — comments/docs only, `.rs` logic byte-identical to `732b61f`). No new crate edge (all three crates already dep `editor-state`); `forbidden-dep` unchanged.

This is the **post-menu-arc deep audit's flagged follow-up**, and follows the **`host.rs` precedent** (a file over 1000L *due to tests* gets the annotation in-the-moment, then a later dispatch extracts the tests).

> **Correction (commit `b65f79b`, per Codex review):** the new `menu_tests.rs` header doc comment originally contained the exact literal `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` substring (describing what it retires). That lint is a bare substring scan, so the prose would falsely auto-exempt `menu_tests.rs` if it ever crossed 1000L and leave a live marker hit in the crate this PR clears. Reworded to "prior line-cap split annotation"; `grep -rn SPLIT-EXEMPTION crates/editor-egui-host/src/` is now ZERO. Doc-comment only.

Same module path — `super` resolves to the crate root whether `menu_tests` is inline or a sibling file, so `use super::{build_main_menu_entries, MenuCommandHandoff}` is unchanged. Same 8 tests, byte-identical assertions. No production logic touched.
