title: VIEWMENU-RESETCAMERA-A4: add the View menu (Reset Camera) — substrate + wiring
body:
**Feature wiring** — the **View** menu (Reset Camera), fourth and final beat of the menu-breadth arc (File #291, Edit #295, Play #297). **Unlike the prior three (pure wiring), View needs a NEW runtime action**: today the editor camera is auto-framed ONLY at construction (`mod.rs:1120`); this adds a runtime "frame the live scene" reset and wires the View menu to it. **Product semantics (owner-confirmed): frame the scene**, not a fixed default-pose jump — matching the constructor's existing auto-frame.

> **Correction (commit `6cd222d`, per Codex review):** the EXEC pass widened the menu-set prose only inside its edit regions and missed 5 live source-doc/comment framings still saying "File + Edit + Play" / "all three points" / (wrapped) "File, Edit, or Play" — `lib.rs:354/390/635/801` + `handoff.rs:76`. A **complete multiline whole-crate sweep** widened all of them to File + Edit + Play + View / "all four points"; a re-sweep of **both** editor-egui-host and editor-shell confirms zero stale framings remain. Docs/comments only — the `.rs` logic is byte-identical to the gate-passed `865df1d`.

## What lands
### Substrate — `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`
- **`EditorShell::reset_camera(&mut self)`** — reframe `editor_camera` to the isometric view of the live scene's AABB union (reusing `compute_aabb_union` + `isometric_camera_for_bounds`), falling back to `EditorCameraState::default()` when nothing is frameable. **Infallible.**
- **`current_scene_bounds(&self)`** — sources the live renderable meshes the same way as `render_path.rs` Step 6: `prebuilt_render_meshes` if present, else the CAD `projection.render_mesh_for(entity, cad_world)`. The render hot path (Step 6) is left untouched.

### Wiring
- **`crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`** — declare `editor.main_menu.view`, register `("view.reset_camera", "Reset Camera", Command::ResetCamera)` on the shared `MenuRegistry` (`build_main_menu_entries()` now returns a 4-tuple, one registry resolving all four points); paint a 4th `menu_button("View")`.
- **`crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`** — `drain_and_route_menu_commands` routes `Command::ResetCamera => self.reset_camera()`. **No swallow** — `reset_camera` is infallible (contrast A3's `route_play_button`, whose `handle_button` can return a benign invalid-state error).

## Tests
- **editor-shell**: `reset_camera_frames_prebuilt_meshes` (off-origin mesh → camera reframes to bbox center + canonical isometric direction), `reset_camera_with_no_scene_falls_back_to_default` (fresh shell → eye returns to (3,3,3)), and `menu_reset_camera_command_reframes_via_view` (Command::ResetCamera drains → reframes).
- **editor-egui-host**: `view_menu_registry_resolves_reset_camera` + `view_menu_entries_round_trip_through_the_handoff`.

## Split-exemption (editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs)
The View additions tipped `lib.rs` past 1000L (production ~913L + inline `#[cfg(test)]` ~120L = 1034L), tripping the `split-exemption` lint. Per the **`host.rs` precedent** (HANDOFF.md §plugin-host: a file crossing 1000L *due to tests* gets a `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation in-the-moment; the test-extraction is a *later* dispatch), this adds a `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation at the file head documenting the cohesive host + flagging the clean follow-up (extract the inline test module to a sibling file). No `exemptions.toml` entry — `split-exemption` is a single inline bare flag.

## Scope / verification
- **4 files, 0 `Cargo.toml`** (+223 / −31; the deletions are rustfmt re-wraps of the widened doc/destructure). No new `Command` variant (`ResetCamera` already existed); no new crate edge (both crates already dep `editor-ui`; `editor-shell` already deps `rge-brep-render`); `forbidden-dep` unchanged (7 rules).
- `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor-egui-host --all-targets` + `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean.
- Local gate **VERIFY OK 7/7 + 10/10 arch lints** (`RUST_TEST_THREADS=2` for the 8GB-GPU visual_smoke).
- **Docs reconcile follows** as a separate `MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A4` dispatch (per the established code-PR-then-doc-PR rhythm), recording the menu-breadth arc (File/Edit/Play/View) complete.

title: MENUREGISTRY-PLAYMENU-A3: add the Play menu (Play/Pause/Stop/Step) via MenuRegistry
body:
**Feature wiring** — the **Play** menu (Play / Pause / Stop / Step), third beat of the menu-breadth arc (A1 File #291, A2 Edit #295). **Pure menu/UI wiring**, the audit's #1 next-beat pick: the PIE path is **already runtime-wired** through `EditorShell::handle_button`; this connects the menu's `Command`s to it via the existing host→shell FIFO. **No new action, no new runtime capability, no `Cargo.toml` change.**

> **Correction (commit `bdd9531`, per Codex review):** the EXEC's single-line sweep left several wrapped/variant rustdoc comments describing the menu set as File/Edit-only after Play joined the FIFO (`handoff.rs`, and `lib.rs` "both menus" / "File + Edit" field + accessor docs). A complete **multiline** sweep widened all of them to File+Edit+Play (or "all three" / registry framing); zero bare "File + Edit" / "File or Edit" / "both menus" now remain in either crate. Docs-only — the `.rs` logic is byte-identical to the gate-passed `03edd65`.

## What lands
- **`crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`** — `PLAY_MENU_EXTENSION_POINT = "editor.main_menu.play"`; `build_main_menu_entries()` now returns a 3-tuple `(file, edit, play)` (one `MenuRegistry`, all three points, resolved once); `play_menu_entries` field; `render()` paints a third `menu_button("Play")`. Mirrors A2 exactly.
- **`crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`** — `drain_and_route_menu_commands` routes `Command::PlayStart/PlayPause/PlayStop/PlayStep` → `handle_button(ToolbarButtonId::Play/Pause/Stop/Step)` via a small `route_play_button` helper.
- **`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`** — 3 new `menu_routing` tests.

## Command → ToolbarButtonId
| Command | button | label |
|---|---|---|
| `PlayStart` | `Play` | "Play" |
| `PlayPause` | `Pause` | "Pause" |
| `PlayStop` | `Stop` | "Stop" |
| `PlayStep` | `Step` | "Step" |

(No item for `FrameStep` — no `Command` variant.)

## Design notes
- **Static items + benign swallow.** The 4 items are STATIC (always present/clickable), so an item can be activated in a `PlayState` where its transition is a no-op (e.g. Stop while `Editing` → `PlayStateError::NoSnapshot`). `handle_button` returns `Err` **before** mutating, so `route_play_button` swallows it (debug log) — exactly mirroring the Space/Escape swallow in `handle_playback_command`. A future dynamic-predicate pass would grey these out instead.
- **No dynamic label.** A Play⇄Pause toggle label needs the deferred per-frame re-resolve; v0 ships fixed labels.

## Tests
- `menu_play_start_command_starts_pie` — `PlayStart` drains → `handle_button(Play)` → Editing→Playing.
- `menu_play_pause_step_stop_round_trip_via_pie` — exercises all 4: Pause→Paused, Step stays Paused, Stop→Editing.
- `menu_play_stop_while_editing_is_a_swallowed_noop` — invalid-state click stays Editing.
- Plus 2 host-side registry/round-trip tests in `editor-egui-host`.

## Scope / verification
- **4 files, 0 `Cargo.toml`** (`editor-egui-host/src/{lib.rs,handoff.rs}` + `editor-shell/src/{render_path.rs,lifecycle/tests.rs}`; no new crate edge — both crates already dep `editor-ui`; `editor-shell` owns `ToolbarButtonId`). `forbidden-dep` unchanged.
- `cargo check` + `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean (both crates); local gate **VERIFY OK 7/7 + 10/10 arch lints** (`RUST_TEST_THREADS=2`).
- **Docs reconcile follows** as a separate `MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A3` dispatch (per the A1→A2 code-PR-then-doc-PR pattern), referencing this PR's merged SHA. View becomes the lone remaining deferred menu.

title: MENUREGISTRY-EDITMENU-A2: add the Edit menu (Undo/Redo) via MenuRegistry
body:
**Option A / step A2** (menu breadth): add an **Edit menu (Undo, Redo)** driven by the data-driven `MenuRegistry`, routed through the existing host→shell FIFO to the bus undo/redo — **behaviour-identical to the existing `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y` path**. 2 source files + 1 test file; **0 `Cargo.toml`**.

## Why Undo/Redo only
The A2 scoping pass established the clean criterion: wire only menu items with **both a `Command` variant AND a reachable shell action**. Only Undo (`undo_command`) and Redo (`redo_command`) qualify today. **View/camera, Play, and clipboard are deferred** — notably the editor's `Ctrl+2/0/4` are *time-scale* controls, not camera, and there is no `reset_camera` action, so a View menu would require building a new action first (its own later dispatch).

## What changed
**`editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`**
- `build_file_menu_entries` → **`build_main_menu_entries`**: ONE `MenuRegistry` now declares **both** `editor.main_menu.file` + `editor.main_menu.edit`, registers File (Open/Save/Save-As, unchanged) + Edit (Undo/Redo), resolves **once** against an empty `PredicateContext`, and returns both projected `(label, Command)` lists. Default `OrderHint::AtEnd` ⇒ registration order; File labels byte-identical to A1.
- `EguiHost` gains `edit_menu_entries` (cached alongside `file_menu_entries`); `new()` destructures the builder into both.
- `render()` borrows both as disjoint fields and renders a second `menu_button("Edit")` after "File" (same `Panel::top` → `MenuBar::new().ui`).
- `menu_tests`: add Edit resolve + FIFO round-trip tests; File tests retargeted onto the `(file, edit)` tuple.

**`editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`**
- `drain_and_route_menu_commands`: add `Command::Undo => undo_command()` and `Command::Redo => redo_command()`, **mirroring `handle_key_command` exactly** (swallow `NothingToUndo`/`NothingToRedo`, warn on other `BusError`). Drain doc + the unrouted-fallback message updated to include Edit Undo/Redo.

**`editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`**
- `menu_routing`: add `menu_undo_command_reverts_via_bus` + `menu_redo_command_reapplies_via_bus` (bus dirty ↔ saved-point transitions prove the menu reaches `undo_command`/`redo_command`).

## Codex TASK-review clarifications (both folded in)
1. The stale A1 "per-frame re-resolve deferred to **A2**" comments → **"future dispatch"** (A2 keeps resolve-once).
2. `menu_unrouted_command_is_noop` switched from `Command::Undo` (now routed) → **`Command::Cut`** (still deferred).

## Pins honored
`from_key_press` untouched; **no** shortcuts/`AcceleratorTable`/plugin entries/dynamic predicates/per-frame re-resolve; View/Play/clipboard/Reset-Camera deferred. Existing File route tests + `render_frame_drains_menu_commands_at_its_top` unchanged.

## Verification
- Canonical gate from the worktree → **VERIFY OK 7/7** + **10/10 arch lints** (new host Edit tests + the two shell route tests pass; `rge-editor`/`editor-shell` recompiled via step-0).

title: GLB-WATCHER-CREATE-EVENT: hot-reload on rename-replace (Create) saves
body:
Lands a previously-uncommitted, test-backed local change (human-authorized salvage; **dispatch 2 of 2** — follows the asset-store fix #293). **`editor/rge-editor/src/glb_watcher.rs` only; 0 `Cargo.toml`.**

## The change
The `--glb` hot-reload watcher converts filesystem events on the active `glb_source_path` into pending reload intent. The old filter accepted **only** `notify::EventKind::Modify(_)`. A user save frequently lands as a **rename-replace** (write a temp file, rename it over the target), which the OS reports as a **`Create`** event for the target path — so rename-replace saves were silently missed (no hot reload).

This widens the filter to accept `Create(_)` too, catching both straight-overwrite (`Modify`) and rename-replace (`Create`) sequences. Sibling events and `Remove`/`Access`/`Other` stay ignored; the debounce window is unchanged.

- `event_targets_path()`: `matches!(event.kind, EventKind::Modify(_) | EventKind::Create(_)) && event.paths.iter().any(|p| p == target)`.
- Module doc ("Event filtering" bullet) + the predicate's rustdoc updated to "a filesystem write landing at the active `--glb` path".
- Test import adds `CreateKind`; new `create_event_for()` helper + test `event_filter_accepts_create_on_target`.

**No new dependency** — `CreateKind` comes from the already-used `notify::event`.

## Verification
- Canonical gate from the worktree → **VERIFY OK 7/7** + **10/10 arch lints**, with `glb_watcher::tests::event_filter_accepts_create_on_target` and the existing modify/sibling filter tests passing. (`rge-editor` is a step-0 path-embedding crate, so its fixture tests recompiled against this worktree.)
- `cargo check -p rge-editor --all-targets` exit 0, **no new warnings** (the lone `egui::TopBottomPanel` deprecation is pre-existing in the transitive `editor-egui-host`); `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean; `git diff --stat` = `editor/rge-editor/src/glb_watcher.rs` only; `git diff --check` clean.

title: ASSET-STORE-WINDOWS-RENAME: AlreadyExists fallback for LocalCache staging renames
body:
Lands a previously-uncommitted, test-backed local change (human-authorized salvage; **dispatch 1 of 2** — the glb_watcher `Create`-event fix follows separately). **`crates/asset-store/src/local.rs` only; 0 `Cargo.toml`.**

## The bug (Windows correctness)
`LocalCache` writes atomically via a staging file + `fs::rename(staging, dest)`. POSIX `rename(2)` atomically **replaces** an existing destination; **Windows `std::fs::rename` instead returns `ErrorKind::AlreadyExists`** when `dest` exists (it maps to `MoveFileEx` *without* `MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING`). On Windows:
- **Index save** — `flush_index()` renames `.index.staging` → `index`. After the first flush the index exists, so **every subsequent explicit flush fails and leaks `.index.staging`**.
- **Payload write** — content-addressed; if the canonical path already exists (another writer won the race for the same asset id), the rename fails and leaks the staging sibling.

## The fix
- **`finish_payload_write(staging, path)`** — on `AlreadyExists` + `path.exists()`, the content-addressed destination names the *same* asset id, so discard the staging file (`remove_file_if_exists`) rather than leak it; other errors propagate. Replaces the inline payload-write rename.
- **`replace_index_with_staging(staging, path)`** — on `AlreadyExists` + `path.exists()`, the index is recoverable, so remove the existing index then re-rename (remove-then-rename fallback); other errors propagate. Replaces the inline index-save rename.
- **`remove_file_if_exists(path, context)`** — helper; `NotFound` treated as Ok.
- Add `ErrorKind` to the `std::io` import.
- **Test** `repeated_flush_replaces_existing_index_without_staging_leak`: two `put` + `flush_index` cycles assert the index contains both ids and `.index.staging` does not leak.

**Unix behaviour is unchanged** — rename replaces, so the `AlreadyExists` arms never fire.

## Verification
- Canonical gate from the worktree → **VERIFY OK 7/7** + **10/10 arch lints**, with `local::tests::repeated_flush_replaces_existing_index_without_staging_leak` passing (it exercises the exact Windows path on the gate host).
- `cargo check -p rge-asset-store --all-targets` exit 0, **no new warnings**; `cargo +nightly fmt --check` clean; `git diff --stat` = `crates/asset-store/src/local.rs` only; `git diff --check` clean.

title: MENUREGISTRY-FILEBAR-A1: registry-drive the File menu bar (behaviour-identical)
body:
**Option A / step A1** from the wire-vs-ratify design report (human-chosen): registry-drive the **existing** File menu bar — **behaviour-identical** — so the data-driven `MenuRegistry` is exercised in production instead of staying dark. `editor-egui-host` only; **single file** (`crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`); **0 `Cargo.toml`**.

## What changed
Swap the File bar's item **production** from the hardcoded `file_menu_items()` array to `MenuRegistry::resolve()`; everything downstream (rendering, the `MenuCommandHandoff` FIFO, the editor-shell routing) is byte-for-byte unchanged.

- New `const FILE_MENU_EXTENSION_POINT = "editor.main_menu.file"` + private `fn build_file_menu_entries() -> Vec<(String, Command)>`: builds a `MenuRegistry`, declares the File point, registers three `MenuEntry` (`file.open`/"Open…", `file.save`/"Save", `file.save_as`/"Save As New Project…"), resolves once against an empty `PredicateContext`, and projects `entries_for(file)` to the `(label, Command)` pairs the bar paints.
- The entries carry the default `OrderHint::AtEnd` in the default section, so `resolve` returns them in **registration order** with **byte-identical labels** → the rendered menu is behaviour-identical to the old list.
- Removed `file_menu_items()` (its content is now the registration source).
- `EguiHost` gains a cached `file_menu_entries: Vec<(String, Command)>`, populated **once** in `EguiHost::new` (resolve-once; per-frame re-resolve deferred to A2).
- `render()` iterates the cached entries (borrowed as a disjoint field before the `run_ui` closure so the closure doesn't capture all of `self`) instead of `file_menu_items()`; the egui structure (`Panel::top` → `MenuBar::new().ui` → `menu_button("File")`), `ui.button(label)` → `push(Command)` → `ui.close()` are unchanged.
- `menu_tests` retargeted onto `build_file_menu_entries()`: (1) it resolves to exactly `[("Open…",OpenFile),("Save",Save),("Save As New Project…",SaveAs)]` in order; (2) those Commands round-trip FIFO through a `MenuCommandHandoff`.

## Pins honored (verbatim from the decision)
- `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` stays the **live keyboard decoder** — untouched.
- **No shortcuts** registered → `AcceleratorTable` stays empty/unused → **no accelerator execution** (display/conflict population is A3).
- **File menu only** (Open / Save / Save As New Project).
- **FIFO handoff + shell routing** (`drain_and_route_menu_commands`, `render_path.rs`) **unchanged**.
- No Edit/View/Play menus, plugin entries, accelerator execution, or registry-wide redesign. **No commitment to A2–A4.**

## Behaviour-identity falsification
The editor-shell route tests (`menu_routing::*`, incl. `render_frame_drains_menu_commands_at_its_top`) are **unchanged** and must still pass — they exercise the FIFO drain/route the host feeds, proving the downstream contract is intact. The host still pushes the same three Commands in the same order onto the same FIFO.

## Verification
- Canonical gate from this worktree → **VERIFY OK 7/7** + **10/10 arch lints**.
- `cargo check -p rge-editor-egui-host --all-targets` clean — **no new warnings** (the one `egui::TopBottomPanel` deprecation is pre-existing, on the untouched bottom status bar).
- `cargo +nightly fmt` clean; `git diff --stat` = `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` only; `git diff --name-only -- '**/Cargo.toml'` empty; `git diff --check` clean.

## Known out-of-scope doc drift → follow-up reconcile (NOT in this PR)
Per the human-pinned single-file scope (and Codex's "keep A1 single-file unless the TASK is amended" guardrail), the living docs that describe the pre-A1 state are **left for a separate doc-reconcile dispatch** (the #289 pattern). Now-stale framing — File bar as "a **direct host menu bar** over a hardcoded `file_menu_items()` list" with "`MenuRegistry::resolve` deferred":
- `plans/BASELINE.md` lines **563, 574, 593, 648**
- `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md` (the "direct host menu bar" framing)

The reconcile should reword these to: the File menu's items are now `MenuRegistry::resolve`-produced and the registry **is** instantiated at `EguiHost::new`; what **remains** deferred is registry-driven command **execution** (still FIFO-routed), accelerator-table execution, Edit/View/Play menus, and plugin entries. Flagged here so the drift is explicit, not an oversight.

title: GATE-STEP0-CLEAN-SET: widen step-0 poison auto-clean 8->10 (arch-lints + editor)
body:
Dispatch-tooling fix: widen the gate's **step-0 shared-target poison auto-clean from 8 → 10 crates**. **`.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` only; no engine source; 0 `Cargo.toml`.**

## Why
Step-0 (linked-worktree-only) `cargo clean -p`'s the `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`-embedding crates so they recompile against the live worktree path (defeats stale-path poisoning from the shared `A:\RustCache\target`). The set was **8**, and the comment had **"intentionally excluded"** `rge-editor` + `rge-tool-architecture-lints` "to keep the per-dispatch refresh cheap." That exclusion let **PR #288** falsely fail — `rge-tool-architecture-lints`'s `forbidden_dep::tests::current_workspace_passes_all_rules` walks up from `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` to the workspace root, so a poisoned binary panics `expected workspace root Cargo.toml at <dead path>` (recurred on #289). The robust rule is **clean every embedding crate, no per-crate exceptions** — the per-crate "is this one harmful?" judgement is precisely what let #288 through.

## What changed (`.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`)
- `$bakingPkgs` **8 → 10**: added `rge-tool-architecture-lints` + `rge-editor`.
- Regen-glob comment widened to `git grep -l CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR -- "crates/**" "tools/**" "editor/**" "runtime/**"`.
- Rationale rewritten: `rge-tool-architecture-lints` = the proven gate-breaker (workspace-root discovery); `rge-editor`'s `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` uses are **all inside its `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`** (`main.rs` ~1698/1757/1798/1844) — golden-project (`golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project`) + io-gltf fixture (`cube.glb`) path reads that **step-4 `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` compiles and runs**, so it's a real fixture-class poisoning vector, **not launch logic**.
- Step-0 summary line: "fixture crate(s)" → "path-embedding crate(s)".

## Verification
- **Verified embedding set:** `git grep -l CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR -- crates tools editor runtime` = 19 files in **exactly 10 crates** (the 8 + arch-lints + editor; no 11th).
- **Queue runner unchanged:** `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` only `git worktree prune`s (no mirrored clean list) — confirmed, no change needed.
- Canonical gate run **from this worktree** → step-0 reports "refreshed 10 path-embedding crate(s)", `current_workspace_passes_all_rules ... ok` (the formerly-poisoned test, rebuilt against this worktree's path), **VERIFY OK 7/7 + 10/10 arch lints**. `git diff --stat` = `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` only; `git diff --check` clean.
- Memory note `rge_shared_target_worktree_cache` records the 8→10 finding (consistent).

## Update — round 2 (per Codex #290 review, NEEDS_CORRECTION)
Codex correctly flagged the original `rge-editor` rationale as false (it claimed "launch logic in `src/main.rs`"). Verified directly: `fn main` is at `main.rs:1096`, `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` at 1299, and all four `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` uses (1698/1757/1798/1844) are inside that test module — golden-project / fixture reads. Corrected the comment + this body + the commit message. The fix is **comment-text only** in `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`; no functional/code line changed (the `$bakingPkgs` 10-crate array, glob comment, and summary string are byte-identical to the round-1 commit that passed the full gate). The edited script was re-validated via PowerShell AST parse (0 errors).

title: MENUBAR-FILE-WIRING: shell drains the menu FIFO + routes Command one-way (Dispatch B)
body:

Called at the **top of `render_frame`** (the sole redraw entry — one drain covers both the full and `render_frame_egui_only` paths) *before* this frame's surface/window/encoder borrows, so the `&mut self` handlers don't contend with a live `&self` borrow.
- **`lifecycle/tests.rs`** — **4 separate per-command route tests** (Open swaps world / Save invokes writer + marks saved / Save-As adopts a Project source / unrouted `Undo` no-op), reusing the existing mock hooks.

title: MENUBAR-FILE-SUBSTRATE: host File menu bar + MenuCommandHandoff FIFO (Dispatch A)
body:
**Frontier step 3 — Dispatch A of 2** (the host-side substrate; Dispatch B wires the editor-shell drain + one-way routing). Begins making the shipped **Open / Save / Save-As** authoring loop **discoverable** via a File menu instead of keyboard-only. **`editor-egui-host` only; 0 `Cargo.toml`; no `editor-shell` changes.**

> **Scope — read this first:** Dispatch A only makes the File menu **visible** and **enqueues** `Command`s. There is **no functional menu effect yet** — nothing drains the queue until the editor-shell drain + routing lands in **Dispatch B**.

## What changed
- **`handoff.rs` — `MenuCommandHandoff`**: a host→shell **FIFO** command queue (bounded `VecDeque<Command>` under one `Mutex`; `push`/`drain` take the same lock; cap 64, drop-newest + warn on overflow). Deliberately a different shape from the latest-only `Handoff<T>` aliases (`InspectorHandoff`/`SaveStatusHandoff`) — menu clicks are **events** that must not overwrite one another.
- **`lib.rs`**: `EguiHost` owns `Arc<MenuCommandHandoff>` (constructed like the other handoffs), a `menu_command_handoff()` accessor, and a re-export. `EguiHost::render` draws a **top egui menu bar** (File: Open / Save / Save As New Project) before the bottom status bar; activating an item enqueues the matching existing `Command`. `file_menu_items()` is the single source of truth (shared by the bar + a test).

## Authority / dependency guardrails (as pinned)
- Reuses the existing **`editor-ui::menus::Command`** — **no new command enum**.
- "Save As New Project…" is **labelled** so but enqueues the existing `Command::SaveAs`; **Dispatch B** routes `Command::SaveAs` → `EditorShell::handle_save_as_new_project_request`.
- **`MenuRegistry::resolve` deferred.** **No new dep edge** (`editor-egui-host` already deps `editor-ui`).
- New menu code uses the current egui APIs (`Panel::top` / `MenuBar::new().ui` / `ui.close`) — **zero new deprecation warnings**. The adjacent bottom status panel keeps its pre-existing `egui::TopBottomPanel` deprecation (present on `main`; migrating it is out of scope).

## Tests (host-owned only — Dispatch A)
- `MenuCommandHandoff`: FIFO order + empties on drain; bounded (drop-newest) cap.
- `file_menu_items()`: offers exactly Open / Save / Save-As-new-project in order; each enqueues its `Command` and they drain FIFO.
- The egui paint (`.button().clicked()` → `push`) is not unit-tested — no egui frame harness in the workspace, consistent with the existing untested `render()`.

## Verification
- `git diff --stat` = exactly the 2 `editor-egui-host` files; `git diff --name-only -- '**/Cargo.toml'` empty; `git diff --check` clean.
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)** + **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)** (host-only; `forbidden-dep` unaffected). Host warm-gate recipe (`RUST_TEST_THREADS=2` + `--jobs 1` bench pre-warm).

title: NEWPROJECT-SAVE-WIRING: Ctrl+Shift+S Save-As to a new .rge-project tree
body:
**Dispatch B — the wiring half of the Save-As feature** (mirrors #274 PROJECT-SAVE-WIRING) over the #283 substrate `save_world_as_new_project`. **editor-shell + `rge-editor` binary + tests; 0 `Cargo.toml`.** editor-shell stays **loader-free / rfd-free** (`Box<dyn …>` only — `forbidden-dep` rule 7 intact).

## What
A `Ctrl+Shift+S` Save-As that creates a fresh `.rge-project` tree and adopts it as the live save source.

**editor-shell**
- **New seams** (`save_request.rs`): `NewProjectSaveDialog { pick_new_project_dir }` + `NewProjectSaveHook { save_world_as_new_project -> created .rge-project path }` (binary-owned).
- **Handler** `handle_save_as_new_project_request` (+ `create_new_project_world` helper): PIE-gated; **cancel / no dialog / no hook / hook error** all log + no-op (mirrors `handle_save_request`). On success **adopts** `SaveSource::Project { path: <created>, name: <folder-derived> }` and marks saved. A non-UTF-8 dir → `name: None` and **the save still succeeds** (path is the source of truth; `display_name` falls back to it).
- **Key** (`commands.rs`): `EditorKeyCommand::SaveAsProject`; `from_key_press` gains a `ctrl && shift` branch (Ctrl+Shift+S) checked *before* the no-Shift set — additive, `Ctrl+S` unchanged; `handle_key_command` arm.
- Fields + `None` defaults (×3 constructors) + `with_new_project_save_dialog` / `with_new_project_save_hook` builders + re-exports (`mod.rs`, `lib.rs`).

**`rge-editor` binary** — `NewProjectSaveFileDialog` (`rfd::FileDialog::pick_folder`) + `NewProjectSaveWriterHook` (over `rge_scene_loader::save_world_as_new_project`), attached in all 3 launch modes.

## Tests (`tests.rs`, additive)
Success (adopt Project + folder name + `display_name` + mark saved); cancel; missing dialog; missing hook; hook error; **Ctrl+Shift+S decode** (and Ctrl+S unchanged); **adopt-then-Ctrl+S** routes through the existing `ProjectSaveHook`. The existing Ctrl+S (Scene/Project silent) and `None`→`.rge-scene` Save-As tests stay green, **unchanged**.

## Verification
- New wiring tests pass (8/8 filtered); both crates `cargo check --all-targets` clean; **0 `Cargo.toml`**.
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)** + **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)** (incl. `forbidden-dep` rule 7 — editor-shell still loader-free).

**This closes the Save-As feature** (substrate #283 + this wiring). Reviewer: OpenAI Codex (execute-then-review). Merge gated on explicit human authorization.

title: NEWPROJECT-SAVE-SUBSTRATE: rge-scene-loader writer that creates a new .rge-project tree
body:
**Dispatch A of the carried Save-As feature** — the substrate half (mirrors #273 PROJECT-SAVE-SUBSTRATE / #267 SCENE-SAVE-SUBSTRATE). **`rge-scene-loader` only; 0 `Cargo.toml`**; no editor-shell / rfd / keybinding (that's Dispatch B).

## Why
`rge_scene_loader::save_project_world_to_path` only **overwrites an existing** `.rge-project` (reads + parses the manifest, resolves `scenes.first()`; its doc says verbatim *"creating a NEW project tree (Save-As) is a follow-up"*). This adds the **create-the-tree** writer.

## What
`pub fn save_world_as_new_project(world, project_dir) -> Result<PathBuf, NewProjectWorldSaveError>`:
- **Tree:** `<dir>/.rge-project` + `<dir>/scenes/main.rge-scene` (reuses `save_scene_world_to_path` for the scene write).
- **Manifest defaults:** folder-derived `name`, `V0_1_0`, `target_tiers: [Desktop]`, no plugins, `scenes: ["scenes/main.rge-scene"]`, empty description.
- **No clobber:** errors *before any write* if either `<dir>/.rge-project` **or** `<dir>/scenes/main.rge-scene` already exists — never overwrites an existing project or a stray user file in a non-project folder.
- **Returns** the created `.rge-project` path (so a caller can adopt it as the new save source); the layout round-trips through `load_scene_world_from_path` by construction.
- New `NewProjectWorldSaveError` (7 variants, `thiserror`, mirroring `ProjectWorldSaveError`).

## Tests — new `tests/new_project_save_round_trip.rs` (mirrors `project_save_round_trip.rs` helpers)
1. **Manifest-field defaults** — name folder-derived, `V0_1_0`, `[Desktop]`, `[]`, single scene path, empty description.
2. **Round-trip** — create from the golden world → `load_scene_world_from_path` → entity ids + 4 component values preserved.
3. **Existing-manifest refusal** — `ProjectAlreadyExists`; no scene created; manifest byte-unchanged.
4. **Existing-scene refusal** — `SceneAlreadyExists`; no manifest written; the user file byte-unchanged.

## Verification
- 4 new tests pass; `git diff --stat` = `src/lib.rs` + the new test file; **0 `Cargo.toml`**.
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)** + **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)**.

**Dispatch B** (NEWPROJECT-SAVE-WIRING) will wire `NewProjectSaveDialog` / `NewProjectSaveHook`, the Ctrl+Shift+S `EditorKeyCommand::SaveAsProject` decode + handler, and the `SaveSource::Project { path, name }` switch over this writer.

title: PROJECT-NAME-DISPLAY-FOLLOWUP: cover open→hook→name flow + distinct-name loader test + fix stale folder-name prose
body:
Audit **P3** — the #279 / **PROJECT-NAME-DISPLAY** loose ends surfaced by the deep audit. **Tests + doc comments + one test fixture only; no product behavior change, 0 `Cargo.toml`.**

## 1. Finding 4 — cover the open→hook→name flow (the real gap)
PR #279 wired `SceneOpenHook::project_display_name` so opening a `.rge-project` threads its manifest name into `SaveSource::Project { name }` (`open_request.rs:307-318`). That plumbing had **no** editor-shell test: the mock hook never overrode `project_display_name`, and the existing `display_name`/snapshot tests construct the variant **by hand**, bypassing the wiring.
- New `NamingSceneOpenHook` (overrides `project_display_name`).
- **Positive** `project_open_threads_hook_display_name_into_save_source`: drives `handle_open_request()` on `/projects/my-game/.rge-project`; asserts the committed source is a Project whose `display_name() == "My Cool Game"` (**≠** folder `my-game`, proving the hook name was threaded) and that the manifest name reaches `save_status_snapshot()`.
- **Negative** `project_open_without_hook_name_falls_back_to_folder`: the default (`None`) hook → folder fallback through the real open path.

## 2. Finding 7 — distinct-name loader test
`read_project_name_returns_golden_manifest_name` asserts `Some("simple-scene")`, but the golden's **folder** is *also* `simple-scene` — so it can't distinguish a manifest-field read from a folder-name read. New tracked fixture `crates/rge-scene-loader/tests/fixtures/distinct-name-project/.rge-project` (manifest `name = "Distinct Display Name"` ≠ folder `distinct-name-project`) + a test pinning `read_project_name` returns the manifest name **and not** the folder. No new dependency (`tempfile` isn't a workspace dep), no `golden-projects/` coupling.

## 3. Stale prose (doc comments)
Post-#279 a `.rge-project` displays its **manifest** name (folder is the fallback), but several docs still framed the folder name as primary — corrected in `window_title.rs`, the editor-shell `save_status_snapshot` doc (`mod.rs`), and the **canonical** `editor-state::SaveStatusSnapshot` type/field docs. The genuinely-accurate *fallback* prose (`main.rs`, `open_request.rs`, `save_source.rs`, the test comments) is left untouched.

## Verification
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)**, **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)**.
- New tests green: both editor-shell open→hook tests + the scene-loader distinct-name test.
- **5 source files + 1 new fixture, 0 `Cargo.toml`**, no product behavior change.

This closes the PROJECT-NAME-DISPLAY (#279) audit loose ends (Findings 4 & 7 + the post-#279 stale prose). Status-doc reconciliation (P2) and Save-As remain separately deferred.

Reviewer: OpenAI Codex (execute-then-review). Merge gated on explicit human authorization.

title: ARCH-LINT-EDITOR-BOUNDARIES: revive dead editor-state-ownership Part B + add editor-shell loader-free rule
body:
Audit **P1** — two architecture-lint fixes surfaced by the 6-dispatch deep audit. **Lint/policy only; no product behavior change** (0 `crates/`/`editor/`/`kernel/` source edits, 0 `Cargo.toml`).

## 1. Revive the dead `editor-state-ownership` Part B (HIGH / L1)
Part B's `FORBIDDEN_IMPORT_PREFIXES` held **bare** names (`"cad_core"`), but real imports are `rge_`-prefixed (`use rge_cad_core::…`) and the leading-segment match was exact — so it **never fired**. The lint reported `PASS` while `editor-state/src/face_selection.rs` really imports `rge_cad_core` types. (Same prefix-mismatch dead-code class as the Audit-5/6 fixes to kernel-isolation, graph-foundation, and forbidden-dep rules 3–6 — Part B was the one missed; `V0_RELEASE_CERTIFICATION.md §1` cites its green status.)

**Fix + policy** (per the human's call — allowlist cad-core ID/tag value types only, not broader authority):
- `normalize_crate_root()` strips an optional leading `rge_` before comparison.
- A cad-core **ID/tag allowlist** `{BRepOwnerId, BRepFaceId, CuboidFaceTag}` — all verified pure identifier/tag value types (opaque 16-byte identity handles + a 6-variant face-direction enum) — is permitted. Leaf-inspection flags any *other* cad-core import (an authority type, or a glob that can't be verified). Every other forbidden crate stays a whole-crate ban.

## 2. Add `forbidden-dep` rule 7 — editor-shell loader-free (MEDIUM / L2)
The load-bearing invariant of the #274–#279 save subsystem — `editor-shell` must not depend on `rge-scene-loader` / `rge-data` (the binary owns the loader edge via the `SceneOpenHook`/save-hook seam) — was convention-only. Rule 7 enforces it on the manifest dep graph, closing the drift vector.

## Tests
- `editor_state_ownership_test` **+5**: a `rge_cad_core` authority import **fails** (proves the dead-code fix); the `{BRepFaceId, BRepOwnerId}` + `CuboidFaceTag` allowlists **pass** (the real production imports); a mixed import flags **only** the authority type; a glob **fails**.
- `forbidden_dep` **+5**: rule-7 pure-logic fixtures + a synthetic-workspace end-to-end; the real-workspace canary renamed `current_workspace_passes_all_rules`.

## Verification
- **Real-workspace lint run: all 10 lints `PASS (0 violations)`** — `editor-state-ownership` now *genuinely* passes (allowlist covers `face_selection.rs`), `forbidden-dep` rule 7 holds.
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)**, **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)**.
- 3 files (lint tool + tests), **0 Cargo.toml**, no new lint (still 10), no `main.rs` change.

P3 (the #279 loose ends) follows next.

Reviewer: OpenAI Codex (execute-then-review). Merge gated on explicit human authorization.

title: PROJECT-NAME-DISPLAY: show a .rge-project's manifest name, not its folder
body:
Shows a `.rge-project`'s declared **manifest `name`** (`rge_data::Project.name`) in the window title + bottom status bar, instead of the project **folder name**. Falls back to the folder name when the manifest name is absent / unreadable / empty. The last larger follow-up from the #274–#278 save-source arc.

## Why it needed threading (not a one-liner)
The manifest `name` wasn't in hand at either `SaveSource::Project` construction site — both the launch path and in-app Ctrl+O build from just the path, and the parsed manifest is discarded inside the loader / `SceneOpenHook` (both returned only a `World`). editor-shell is deliberately **loader-free**, so it can't parse `rge-data` itself, and a per-frame re-read in `display_name()` is unacceptable.

## Design (lean, additive, loader-free)
- **`rge-scene-loader`** — new `read_project_name(path) -> Option<String>` (additive; reuses existing `rge-data` + `ron` deps). `None` on any I/O/parse failure → folder fallback. World-free: a one-shot Open/launch read, **not** per-frame.
- **`SceneOpenHook`** — additive **defaulted** `project_display_name(&path) -> Option<String> { None }`, so the mock + all 8 existing call sites compile unchanged; the binary-owned loader hook overrides it.
- **`SaveSource::Project(PathBuf)` → `Project { path, name: Option<String> }`** — the name lives *in* the variant (no parallel `EditorShell` field — that would reintroduce the two-sources smell #274 removed). `display_name()` prefers a present, non-empty name, else the existing folder fallback.
- **Construction sites thread the name** — in-app Ctrl+O via the hook (`open_request.rs`), `--scene` launch via `read_project_name` (`main.rs`).

`load_scene_world_from_path`'s signature is **untouched** (it has ~14 callers); the additive helper exists precisely to avoid that ripple.

## Scope / safety
**7 files across 3 crates, 0 `Cargo.toml`** — editor-shell gains **no** `rge-data` / `rge-scene-loader` dependency (the `forbidden-dep` / `kernel-isolation` lints confirm the loader-free boundary held). An empty manifest name falls back to the folder so a blank title is never rendered.

## Tests
- `read_project_name`: golden `.rge-project` → `"simple-scene"`; missing path → `None`.
- `display_name`: prefers the manifest name; falls back to the folder when `None`/empty; bare `.rge-project` → file name.
- Status snapshot end-to-end: a named project surfaces its name; an unnamed one its folder.
- Existing PROJECT-SAVE-WIRING tests carry `name: None`.

## Verification
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)**, **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)**, doctests + bench-no-run clean.

Reviewer: OpenAI Codex (execute-then-review). Merge gated on explicit human authorization.

title: KEYCOMMAND-SAVE-RENAME: rename EditorKeyCommand::MarkSaved -> Save
body:
Renames `EditorKeyCommand::MarkSaved` → **`EditorKeyCommand::Save`**, the last of the three carried follow-ups from the #274–#277 save-source run.

## Why
The `Ctrl+S` variant's name predated its behavior. Originally it only marked the Command Bus cursor as the saved point (`command_bus.mark_saved()`). **SCENE-SAVE-WIRING** repointed the `handle_key_command` arm to `self.handle_save_request()`, so the variant now performs a **real save** — write the live `World` to the open `.rge-scene` / `.rge-project` on disk, then mark the bus saved point on success. The variant's own doc comment already flagged the rename as a deferred follow-up; this lands it. So the rename is **semantically accurate**, not just cosmetic.

## What changed (4 files, editor-shell-internal)
- **`commands.rs`** — variant `MarkSaved` → `Save` (enum def, `from_key_press`, the `handle_key_command` arm, the key-map doc table); the variant doc comment is rewritten to describe `Save` and drops the now-satisfied "rename is a deferred follow-up" sentence.
- **`save_request.rs`** — 4 intra-doc links / prose mentions.
- **`lifecycle/tests.rs`** + **`tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`** — the doc/comments, the dispatch calls, and the `from_key_press` expected value.

## Deliberately NOT renamed
`EditorShell::mark_saved_command()` and the bus op `command_bus.mark_saved()` — those are genuine *mark-the-bus-cursor-saved* operations; their names are accurate. Only the `EditorKeyCommand` **variant** is renamed.

## Scope / safety
Pure identifier + prose rename — no behavior change. The enum is `#[derive(Debug, …)]` only (no serde), so no wire impact; no test asserts on the `"MarkSaved"` Debug string. `rg 'MarkSaved' crates` is empty (the only repo-wide hits are immutable `ai_handoffs/` packets). 0 `Cargo.toml`.

## Verification
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)**, **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)** — incl. the keyboard round-trip + Ctrl+S writer-routing tests against the renamed variant.

Reviewer: OpenAI Codex (execute-then-review). Merge gated on explicit human authorization.

- **Field rename** — `SaveStatusSnapshot::scene_file_name` → **`source_name`** (editor-state definition + docs, the editor-shell `save_status_snapshot()` producer, the editor-ui widget read, and all tests).
- **Empty-state string** — `save_status_line`'s `None` arm `"No scene"` → **`"No file"`** (the `None` case = no save source of any kind; a `.rge-project` isn't a "scene"). The `" *"` dirty marker is unchanged, so the status bar and OS title still read consistently.
- **Test wording** — the loosely-scene-named fns/comments in the same surface ported (`no_scene_*` → `no_source_*` / `no_file`); the genuinely scene-specific tests (which exercise `SaveSource::Scene`) keep their names.

(The lone repo-wide hit, `plans/BASELINE.md`'s historical "No scene/project persistence" finding, is a coincidental substring about a past baseline state — unrelated, left as-is.)

- Updated editor-state / editor-shell / editor-ui save-status tests pass.

`source_name` / `"No file"` are sensible neutral defaults — trivially swappable (e.g. `"No document"`) if you prefer a different word.

title: GITIGNORE-VERIFY-TARGET-DIR: gitignore the verify-gate's target-local-verify build dir
body:
Gitignores the `target-local-verify/` cargo target directory that the verify-gate environment produces inside dispatch worktrees, closing a `git add -A` footgun.

## Why
The existing `/target/` + `**/target/` rules match a dir named exactly `target`, not `target-local-verify`. During SAVE-SOURCE-DISPLAY-NAME (#275) a routine `git add -A` swept ~1205 `target-local-verify/**` build-artifact files into a commit (caught + reset before push). Every dispatch worktree that accumulates this dir is exposed to the same near-miss.

## What
Added under the `# Build outputs` section:
```gitignore
/target-local-verify/
**/target-local-verify/
```
mirroring the existing `/target/` + `**/target/` pair.

## Scope & investigation
- **`.gitignore` only**, +4 lines. `git grep target-local-verify` matches no committed file — it's local gate/review tooling output, so there is no script to edit. No currently-tracked file is affected (`git ls-files | rg target-local-verify` empty).
- Deliberately **not** in scope: the gate's `$bakingPkgs` clean-list (correctly derived from `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` usage — `rge-pak-format` doesn't bake it), and the deeper shared-target cross-worktree poisoning / flaky-launch hardening (separate follow-up).

## Verification
- `git check-ignore -v` matches `**/target-local-verify/` for both root (`target-local-verify/debug/build/x.json`) and nested (`crates/foo/target-local-verify/y`) paths.
- A real `target-local-verify/` dir no longer shows as untracked in `git status`.
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)**, **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)** — a `.gitignore` change can't affect fmt/lints/tests/doctests; the green run confirms no incidental breakage.

title: SAVE-SOURCE-DISPLAY-NAME: SaveSource::display_name() — project title/status show folder name, not literal .rge-project
body:
Polishes the title bar + bottom status bar so an open `.rge-project` reads as its **folder name** (e.g. `my-game`) instead of the literal `.rge-project`. The carried OQ2 follow-up from PROJECT-SAVE-WIRING (#274).

## What changed
- **`SaveSource::display_name(&self) -> Option<&str>`** — a `Scene` shows its `.rge-scene` file name (unchanged); a `Project` shows its parent **folder** name, falling back to the file name (`.rge-project`) when the path has no parent. Pure-path — **no manifest read** (editor-shell stays loader-free), **no `SaveSource` shape change**.
- **Window title** — `editor_window_title` now takes the resolved `Option<&str>` display name (the file-name/folder-name extraction moved into `display_name()`); `sync_window_title` feeds it `save_source().display_name()`.
- **Status bar** — `save_status_snapshot().scene_file_name` is derived from `display_name()`, so a project surfaces its folder name.
- **Stale-prose sweep** — updated the now-false "literal `.rge-project`" / "pre-extracted via `Path::file_name`" title/status prose (editor-shell + editor-state docs). Accurate path/format/open-routing "literal `.rge-project`" references stay.

## Scope
5 files (editor-shell `save_source.rs` / `mod.rs` / `window_title.rs` / `tests.rs` + editor-state doc), **0 Cargo.toml**, no binary change.

## Verification
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)**, **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)**, doctests + bench-no-run clean.
- +6 tests: `display_name` unit cases (scene file name; project folder name; no-parent fallback) + Scene/Project `save_status_snapshot` integration + the window-title pure-fn tests updated to the `Option<&str>` signature. All green.
- Note: a transient `cargo test --workspace` false-negative (shared-target cache-poisoning in 8 unrelated baked-asset crates, from the just-pruned #274 worktree) was cleared via `cargo clean -p`; the re-gate is fully green. No change in those crates.

## Out of scope (carried follow-ups)
- Manifest-`name` display (nicer than the folder name when they differ) — needs threading the name through `SaveSource` at open time.
- Source-neutral `"No scene"` empty-state string + `scene_file_name` field rename.

title: PROJECT-SAVE-WIRING: route Ctrl+S by SaveSource (Scene/Project), replacing scene_source_path
body:
Part 2 of in-app `.rge-project` save (substrate landed in #273). Wires the editor so an open `.rge-project` saves back on `Ctrl+S`, and unifies the editor-shell save-source model.

## What changed
- **Source model.** Replaces editor-shell `scene_source_path: Option<PathBuf>` with a unified `save_source: Option<SaveSource>` where `SaveSource = Scene(PathBuf) | Project(PathBuf)` — a scene-source and a project-source can no longer both be set (illegal state unrepresentable). `.glb` stays separate (a reload/watch source, not a save source).
- **Ctrl+S routing** (`handle_save_request` dispatches on the variant):
  - `Scene` -> `SceneSaveHook` (`save_scene_world_to_path`), silent overwrite
  - `Project` -> **new** `ProjectSaveHook` (`save_project_world_to_path`), silent write (overwrite first scene + manifest)
  - `None` -> Save-As (commits a `Scene` source)
- **Open / `--scene`** commit `Scene` for a `.rge-scene` and `Project` for a literal `.rge-project`.
- **Binary** owns the loader edge via a new `ProjectSaveWriterHook` (mirrors `SceneSaveWriterHook`), attached in all three launch builders. **editor-shell stays free of `rge-scene-loader` / `rge-data` — no Cargo.toml change.**
- Title + status bar derive the file name from `save_source_path()` (v0: the literal `.rge-project` for a project; a friendlier `display_name()` is a noted follow-up).

## Scope
10 files, 0 Cargo.toml. 8 from the TASK MAY-edit set + 2 surfaced `scene_source_path` consumers (`tests/save_status_snapshot_smoke.rs`, `editor-state` doc) ported per the TASK halt condition (port-it-too-and-report). No `scene_source_path` symbol remains.

## Verification
- Canonical gate: **VERIFY OK (7/7)**, **10/10 architecture lints (0 violations)**, doctests + bench-no-run clean.
- New tests: `scene_open_of_rge_project_commits_project_save_source`, `save_with_project_source_routes_to_project_hook`, `project_save_failure_does_not_mark_saved`, `project_save_without_hook_is_noop` (editor-shell), and `project_save_writer_hook_round_trips_via_disk` (binary, real substrate via a temp copy of the golden project). Ported `.rge-scene` tests unchanged -> behavior preserved.

Reviewer: OpenAI Codex (execute-then-review). Merge gated on explicit human authorization.

title: PROJECT-SAVE-SUBSTRATE: save_project_world_to_path (.rge-project writer)
body:
## PROJECT-SAVE-SUBSTRATE — `save_project_world_to_path` (the `.rge-project` writer)

Part 1 of in-app `.rge-project` save. No `.rge-project` writer existed —
`save_scene_world_to_path` rejected it with a *"deferred to a follow-up"* note.
This adds the **serialization-bearing lib substrate** in isolation (where the
round-trip correctness lives), splitting the feature the way scene-save was
split (#267 SCENE-SAVE-SUBSTRATE → #268/#269 wiring).

### What it does
`save_project_world_to_path(world, project_path)` writes a live `World` back to
an **existing** `.rge-project` by overwriting its first scene and re-writing the
manifest. It is the save-side inverse of the `.rge-project` branch of
`load_scene_world_from_path` and **mirrors that reader's resolution exactly** —
exact `.rge-project` file name → `scenes.first()` → project-parent-relative
scene path — so a save → load round-trips by construction. The scene write
**reuses `save_scene_world_to_path`**; the manifest is re-stamped
`SchemaVersion::V0_1_0` and re-serialized.

`ProjectWorldSaveError` carries the distinct failure modes (UnsupportedExtension
/ Read / ParseProject / EmptyProjectScenes / ProjectHasNoParentDir / Scene /
SerializeManifest / WriteManifest). The stale *"deferred to a follow-up"* prose
on the scene writer + its error variant is retired.

### Scope (2 files, one crate)
- `crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs` — `ProjectWorldSaveError` + `save_project_world_to_path` + doc refresh.
- `crates/rge-scene-loader/tests/project_save_round_trip.rs` (NEW) — 5 tests.

**No editor-shell / binary / `Cargo.toml` change, no `unsafe`.** v0 is
overwrite-an-existing-project; Save-As to a new project tree is deferred.

### Tests (headless)
- `save_then_load_project_round_trips_via_disk` — write golden world into a temp project → `load_scene_world_from_path` → component sets preserved; scene file created at the resolved path.
- `manifest_is_preserved_across_save` — the re-written manifest re-parses equal to the original (version `V0_1_0`).
- Rejection paths: non-exact name → `UnsupportedExtension` (before I/O); empty `scenes` → `EmptyProjectScenes`; missing manifest → `Read`.
- Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` green — architecture lints 10/10.

Dispatch protocol: `ai_handoffs/PROJECT-SAVE-SUBSTRATE_*`. Run execute-then-review (Codex reviews this PR post-exec). Follow-up: **PROJECT-SAVE-WIRING** (editor source-model + routing + binary `ProjectSaveHook`). **Human merge authorization required before merge.**

title: GENERIC-LATEST-HANDOFF: unify the three latest-only handoffs into Handoff<T>
body:
## GENERIC-LATEST-HANDOFF — unify the three latest-only handoffs into `Handoff<T>`

`RenderHandoff`, `InspectorHandoff`, and `SaveStatusHandoff` were three
byte-identical hand-written copies of a `Mutex<Option<Arc<T>>>` + `AtomicU64`
latest-only slot. With the third copy landed (Rule of Three), this collapses the
mechanism into **one generic `Handoff<T>`** and keeps the three names as **type
aliases** — so every call site is unchanged.

### Home + alias strategy
- `Handoff<T>` lives in **`rge-editor-state`** — the only crate both
  `editor-shell` and `editor-egui-host` already depend on (no new dep edge, no
  cycle). It's dep-neutral shared infrastructure, **not** a 6th coordination
  category (same precedent as `InspectorSnapshot`/`SaveStatusSnapshot`).
- `type RenderHandoff = Handoff<RenderInputOwned>` (editor-shell),
  `type InspectorHandoff = Handoff<InspectorSnapshot>` and
  `type SaveStatusHandoff = Handoff<SaveStatusSnapshot>` (editor-egui-host).
  The ~60 call sites, the `EditorShell`/`EguiHost` fields, and all external
  tests resolve transparently and were left untouched.

### Governance
- **ADR-117 consistent, no amendment**: the ADR pins *semantics* and
  sub-decision 5 / non-decision 6 explicitly defer the crate/primitive choice.
  Semantics (latest-only/drop-old, immutable, non-blocking, monotonic
  generation) are preserved; the `(ecs_tick, checkpoint_id)` anchor stays a
  `RenderInputOwned` field. No `unsafe`, std-only.
- **Verbatim-copy doctrine retired** in prose across `render_input.rs`,
  `editor-egui-host/src/handoff.rs`, and `editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` (one
  generic definition now serves the auditability the three copies were kept for).

### Scope (5 files, no `Cargo.toml` change)
`editor-state/src/handoff.rs` (NEW, generic + 9-test suite) · `editor-state/src/lib.rs` · `editor-shell/src/render_input.rs` · `editor-egui-host/src/handoff.rs` · `editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` (doc-only).

### Behavior delta (intended, minor)
`Debug`/panic strings become generic `"Handoff"` (verified: no test asserts the
type-name string; the two Debug tests assert only the `"generation: N"`
substring).

### Tests
- New generic mechanism suite: **9/9** (`editor-state::handoff`).
- All ADR-117 boundary tests (`render_input_boundary`) + egui-host
  tab/dock/handoff integration suites pass **unchanged via the aliases**.
- Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` green — architecture lints 10/10.

Dispatch protocol: `ai_handoffs/GENERIC-LATEST-HANDOFF_*` (pre-exec REVIEW: APPROVED, all 5 OQs at defaults). **Human merge authorization required before merge.**

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: EDITOR-SAVE-STATUS-INDICATOR: in-app bottom status bar (scene name + dirty)
body:
## EDITOR-SAVE-STATUS-INDICATOR — in-app bottom status bar (scene name + dirty)

Follow-on to **#270 EDITOR-WINDOW-TITLE**: surfaces the same save state
**inside the editor** as a bottom status bar, not just in the OS title bar.
The editor already tracked `scene_source_path` (#269) and a Command-Bus dirty
flag; this renders them through the existing egui host path.

### Why a separate snapshot/handoff (not extending the inspector)
`InspectorSnapshot` is `#[derive(Copy)]` by **documented hard invariant**, and
a scene name is a non-`Copy` `String` — extending it would break that
invariant, and its doctrine scopes it to coordinator-state, not save/chrome
state. So this mirrors the inspector stack **verbatim** with a separate,
additive channel (the in-repo doctrine: handoffs are copied verbatim so audits
grep the same shape).

### The slice (additive, 4 crates, 10 files = 4 new + 6 edited)
- **editor-state**: new `SaveStatusSnapshot { scene_file_name: Option<String>, is_dirty: bool }` (Clone, not Copy).
- **editor-ui**: new `widgets/save_status.rs` — pure `save_status_line()` + `ui()`.
- **editor-egui-host**: new `SaveStatusHandoff` (verbatim `InspectorHandoff` copy) + a bottom `TopBottomPanel` in `EguiHost::render` (the sanctioned "status bars layer into the render path" extension point).
- **editor-shell**: new `save_status_handoff` field + `save_status_snapshot()` producer, published at the two existing per-frame publish sites.

### Held invariants
- `InspectorSnapshot` / `InspectorHandoff` / the inspector widget **unchanged**.
- `EguiHost::render` signature **unchanged** (additive bottom panel + new handoff field).
- **No new crate dependency** (no `Cargo.toml` change), **no binary change**, no kernel/data/scene-loader/runtime change, no ADR/lint/exemption.
- Dirty marker is `" *"` for consistency with the window title; empty slot renders `"No scene"`.

### Tests
- editor-ui formatter: 7 headless cases (4 state combos + default + marker-consistency + single-line).
- `SaveStatusHandoff`: 8 (mirrors the inspector handoff suite).
- editor-shell producer: 8 (file-name extraction, dirty/mark-saved, purity, trait bounds).
- Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` green — architecture lints 10/10, 0 violations.

Dispatch protocol: `ai_handoffs/EDITOR-SAVE-STATUS-INDICATOR_*`. Pre-exec review routed to Codex on this concrete PR (execute-then-review). **Human merge authorization required before merge.**

title: EDITOR-WINDOW-TITLE: reflect open scene + dirty state in title bar
body:
## Summary

Surface the in-app Save state in the OS window title — `level.rge-scene * — RGE Editor` (asterisk = unsaved edits). The editor now tracks a `scene_source_path` (#269) and a Command-Bus dirty flag, but the winit title was a static `"RGE Editor"`; this shows which file `Ctrl+S` writes and whether there are unsaved changes. **Pure editor-shell — no binary change, no new dependency.**

- **New `lifecycle/window_title.rs`** — a pure `editor_window_title(scene_source_path: Option<&Path>, is_dirty: bool) -> String` (the only logic, fully unit-testable) + `EditorShell::sync_window_title`, which pushes the title to the winit window via `set_title` **only when it changed** since the last sync (tracked by a new `last_window_title` field), and no-ops on a windowless (headless) shell.
- **`mod.rs`** — `sync_window_title()` is reconciled once per frame from the `WindowEvent::RedrawRequested` branch; `last_window_title` field + constructor inits.

Mirrors the binary's `glb_watcher_action` / `sync_glb_watcher` pure-decision + reconcile shape.

## Format

`Some(path)` → `"{file_name}{ *} — RGE Editor"`; `None` (demo / `--glb` / no scene) → `"RGE Editor{ *}"`; the `*` appears when dirty.

## Tests

+6 headless editor-shell (`lifecycle/tests.rs`): the four format cases (no-source clean/dirty, with-source clean/dirty), `window_title_uses_file_name_not_full_path`, and `sync_window_title_without_window_is_noop`. The `set_title` side-effect is not headless-testable (the same boundary as `sync_glb_watcher`'s `notify` side), so the pure function carries the behavioral coverage.

## Verification

Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` green — `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`. Focused `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell` green. editor-shell `Cargo.toml` untouched — no new dependency.

## Scope

`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/window_title.rs` (new), `.../lifecycle/mod.rs`, `.../lifecycle/tests.rs`. No binary (`editor/rge-editor`) / kernel / data / loader change; no new dependency.

title: SCENE-SAVE-SOURCE-PATH: true Save (silent overwrite of opened scene)
body:
## Summary

Dispatch 3 of the in-app Save line — gives `Ctrl+S` a **true Save**. When the editor knows the scene's source file (`scene_source_path`), `Ctrl+S` writes straight back to it with **no dialog**; **Save-As** remains the fallback when there's no known source. Lifts the Dispatch-2 (#268) Save-As-only limit. No new editor-shell dependency.

- **editor-shell** — new `scene_source_path: Option<PathBuf>` + `with_scene_source_path` builder + `scene_source_path()` accessor.
  - **Commit**: the scene branch of `handle_open_request` commits it on a successful `.rge-scene` Open; `handle_save_request` commits the picked path on a successful Save-As.
  - **Clear**: `replace_world` resets it (the scene-Open handler re-commits on success).
  - **`handle_save_request` branch**: `Some(src)` → silent overwrite (no dialog); `None` → Save-As → write → commit. All failure / cancel / no-hook / PIE paths no-op with `scene_source_path` + `is_dirty()` untouched.
- **`.rge-scene`-only tracking** — a `.rge-project` Open (or `--scene .rge-project` launch) leaves `scene_source_path` `None`, because the writer (`save_scene_world_to_path`) rejects `.rge-project`; `Ctrl+S` stays Save-As there.
- **rge-editor** — the `--scene` site seeds `scene_source_path` only for a `*.rge-scene` launch (per the pre-exec correction); `.glb` / demo launches don't.

## Protocol

TASK → pre-exec REVIEW (NEEDS_CORRECTION: the `--scene` seed must be `.rge-scene`-conditional to match the "track only `.rge-scene` sources" decision) → CORRECT → EXEC.

## Tests

+7 headless editor-shell (`lifecycle/tests.rs`): `scene_open_commits_scene_source_path`, `scene_open_of_rge_project_leaves_source_path_none`, `save_with_source_path_overwrites_without_dialog` (silent path bypasses a cancel-dialog and writes the tracked path), `save_without_source_path_prompts_and_commits`, `save_as_failure_does_not_commit_source_path`, `replace_world_clears_scene_source_path`, `save_outside_editing_with_source_is_noop`. The existing SCENE-SAVE-WIRING save tests stay green (no source → the now-default Save-As branch, behavior unchanged).

## Verification

Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` green — `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`. Focused `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor` green. editor-shell `Cargo.toml` untouched — no new dependency.

## Scope

`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/{mod.rs, save_request.rs, open_request.rs, tests.rs}`, `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`. No kernel-ecs / rge-data / rge-scene-loader internals; no new trait; no `.rge-project` save.

title: SCENE-SAVE-WIRING: in-app Ctrl+S Save (Save-As, .rge-scene)
body:
## Summary

Dispatch 2 of 2 for in-app Save — the save-side mirror of SCENE-OPEN-WIRING (`Ctrl+O`). Wires `Ctrl+S` to write the live editor `World` to a `.rge-scene` via a binary-owned save dialog + writer hook calling the Dispatch-1 substrate `rge_scene_loader::save_scene_world_to_path`. **Save-As only (v0):** every `Ctrl+S` prompts for a destination; no `scene_source_path` / silent overwrite (a follow-up). No new editor-shell dependency.

- **editor-shell** — new `lifecycle/save_request.rs`: `SceneSaveDialog::pick_save_path` + `SceneSaveHook::save_scene_world(&World, &Path)` traits, and the public, Editing-gated `handle_save_request` (dialog → writer → `mark_saved()` **only on `Ok`**; cancel / no-dialog / no-hook / non-Editing / write-error all warn-log + no-op with `is_dirty()` untouched). `with_scene_save_dialog` / `with_scene_save_hook` builders + boxed-`dyn` fields (preserved across `replace_world`).
- **Ctrl+S wiring** — `Ctrl+S` already decodes to `EditorKeyCommand::MarkSaved`; its `handle_key_command` arm is repointed from `mark_saved_command()` to `handle_save_request()`. The variant name is retained (rename to `Save` deferred as cosmetic); the `WindowEvent::KeyboardInput` branch is untouched.
- **rge-editor** — binary owns `rfd` + `rge-scene-loader`: `SceneSaveFileDialog` (`save_file()`) + `SceneSaveWriterHook` (derives `Scene.name` from the file stem, calls the substrate), attached at all three launch sites.

## v0 contract

`.rge-scene` only (the substrate rejects other extensions); `.rge-project` deferred. `Scene.name` = chosen file stem. Editing-gated like Open (a mid-Play Save would persist the play-state world). editor-shell gains no `rfd` / `rge-scene-loader` / `rge-data` edge.

## Tests

- **+7 headless editor-shell** (`lifecycle/tests.rs`, mock dialog + writer): `save_writes_via_hook_and_marks_saved`, `save_failure_does_not_mark_saved`, `save_cancelled_dialog_is_noop`, `save_without_dialog_is_noop`, `save_without_hook_is_noop`, `save_outside_editing_is_noop`, `ctrl_s_routes_to_save`.
- **+1 binary** (`rge-editor`): `scene_save_writer_hook_round_trips_via_disk` — `SceneSaveWriterHook` save → reload against the golden simple-scene.
- **Updated** `keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`: the `Ctrl+S` step now drives the Save flow (writer invoked + dirty cleared); `key_command_mapping_table_is_exact` is unchanged (variant name retained).

## Protocol

TASK → pre-exec REVIEW (NEEDS_CORRECTION: a truncated-grep current-state claim missed the `MarkSaved` reference in `keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`) → CORRECT (added that test to scope, fixed the claim, required the test update) → EXEC.

## Verification

Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` green — `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.` Focused `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell` (170 passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored) and `-p rge-editor` (76 passed / 0 failed). editor-shell `Cargo.toml` untouched — no new dependency.

## Scope

`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/save_request.rs` (new), `.../lifecycle/mod.rs`, `.../lifecycle/commands.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lib.rs`, `.../lifecycle/tests.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`, `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`. No kernel-ecs / rge-data / rge-scene-loader internals; no `.rge-project`; no `scene_source_path`.

title: SCENE-SAVE-SUBSTRATE: World -> rge-scene extraction + .rge-scene save
body:
## Summary

Dispatch 1 of 2 for in-app Save. Adds the save-side inverse of the loader to `rge-scene-loader` — a tested `World -> rge_data::Scene` extraction plus a `.rge-scene` RON write path — with no editor/binary/runtime/kernel changes and no new dependency.

- `extract_scene_from_world(world, name) -> Result<Scene, SceneSaveError>` — unions the four supported-component queries (Transform / Camera / Light / Visibility), orders entities by ULID ascending for deterministic output, emits components in fixed order. The kernel->data `EntityId` inverse is `rge_data::EntityId::from_u128(id.ulid().0)`.
- `save_scene_world_to_path(world, path, name) -> Result<(), SceneWorldSaveError>` — `.rge-scene`-only writer (rejects the literal `.rge-project` and other names as `UnsupportedExtension`), pretty RON, `std::fs::write`.
- `SceneSaveError` / `SceneWorldSaveError` mirror the existing load-side error taxonomy.
- Shared `TYPE_ID_*` consts are now used by both `insert_component` (decode) and the extractor (encode), so the two directions cannot drift.

## v0 fidelity (documented contract)

Entity IDs + the four supported components only. `Scene.name` is caller-supplied; entity names, relations, and `root_entities` emit empty (the loaded `World` does not retain them); entities carrying none of the four supported components are not written (union-of-queries enumeration).

## Tests

+7 headless tests in `tests/scene_save_round_trip.rs`, seeded from the tracked golden simple-scene fixture (Camera + KeyLight):

- `world_scene_world_value_round_trip` — World→Scene→World preserves entity ids + the four component values (the loader is the spec-conformance anchor), and extract is a fixed point through load.
- `extract_is_deterministic_and_byte_stable`, `empty_world_extracts_empty_scene`, `entities_without_supported_components_are_skipped` (component-less + unsupported-only), `fidelity_contract_emits_empty_names_relations_roots`, `save_then_load_round_trips_via_disk`, `unsupported_extension_is_rejected`.

## Verification

Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` green — `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`: fmt --check, architecture lints + lint-test suite, cargo-deny, `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` (422s, 0 failed), `--doc` (80s), bench --no-run (128s). Focused `cargo test -p rge-scene-loader` = 16 passed / 0 failed.

## Scope

Only `crates/rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs` (+243/−14) and the new test file. `Cargo.toml` untouched (no new dependency). No `editor-shell` / binary / `runtime-headless` / `kernel-ecs` change. The `.rge-project` write/update and `Ctrl+S` + `SceneSaveHook` wiring are Dispatch 2.

title: SCENE-OPEN-WIRING: in-app Ctrl+O scene Open + GLB watcher teardown
body:
## Summary

Dispatch 2 of the human-directed in-app scene-Open split (Dispatch 1 = EDITOR-WORLD-SWAP, #265). This is the **wiring** on top of the two merged substrates — `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_world_from_path` (#264) and `EditorShell::replace_world` (#265): `Ctrl+O` can now open a `.rge-scene` / `.rge-project` from inside the running editor.

- **editor-shell** gains a `SceneOpenHook` trait (binary-owned, like `AssetReloadHook` / `GlbOpenDialog`), a `scene_open_hook` field + `with_scene_open_hook` builder, and an **extension dispatch** in `handle_open_request`:
  - `.rge-scene` / `.rge-project` -> load via the hook into a fresh kernel `World`, then `replace_world` (Editing-gated, blanks the viewport, clears `glb_source_path`). The load runs in the hook **before** the swap, so a malformed scene leaves the live world untouched — the scene analogue of the GLB commit-after-success property.
  - `.glb` -> existing render-swap path, verbatim.
  - anything else -> warn + no-op.
- **binary** (`rge-editor`): `SceneOpenLoaderHook` (wraps `load_scene_world_from_path`); the rfd dialog now offers `.glb` + `.rge-scene` + an All-Files filter (so a literal `.rge-project` is pickable) and is retitled Open; the scene hook is attached in all three launch modes (demo / `--glb` / `--scene`).
- **GLB watcher teardown**: the pure decision helper goes from re-root-only `Option<PathBuf>` to a 3-way `GlbWatcherAction { Reroot, Teardown, Unchanged }`. A scene Open clears `glb_source_path`, so `sync_glb_watcher` now **tears the watcher down** (source `None` + watched `Some` -> `Teardown`) — closing the watcher-shutdown caveat carried through #263/#264/#265.

v0 renders the swapped-in scene blank (matching `--scene` / `replace_world`); rendering scene entities is future work, as is in-app Save.

## Dependency boundary (unchanged)

editor-shell gains **no** `rge-scene-loader` / `rge-data` / `rfd` / `notify` dependency — the scene loader, dialog, and watcher all stay binary-owned behind trait objects. `forbidden-dep` stays green; exemption count unchanged.

## Tests

title: EDITOR-WORLD-SWAP: runtime EditorShell::replace_world substrate
body:
## What

Add `EditorShell::replace_world` — a runtime surface to swap the live editor `World` after construction. Dispatch `EDITOR-WORLD-SWAP`; the **substrate** under in-app scene Open (the `Ctrl+O` wiring is a follow-up dispatch). Codex pre-exec review: NEEDS_CORRECTION → CORRECT → approved.

## Why

Today a `World` is only installed at construction (`with_world`); there is no runtime mutator. In-app scene Open must replace the live world while the editor is running. Split out **substrate-first** (per plan) because the swap carries real reset semantics that warrant isolated review before the dialog/loader/watcher wiring.

## How

`pub fn replace_world(&mut self, world: rge_kernel_ecs::World) -> Result<(), String>`, Editing-gated (mirrors `reload_render_assets`), resets the shell to the `with_world` data baseline:
- installs the new kernel world into a fresh wrapper (TimeScale-if-absent);
- clears CAD-mode fields (`cad_world`/`projection`/`cad_graph`/`cad_entity`);
- clears render content (`prebuilt_render_*` + GPU `meshes`/`materials` + `highlight_index_buffer`) → **blank viewport** (`encode_main_pass` skips an empty mesh set — the `--scene` load-only semantics; `reload_render_assets` can't blank, it errors on empty);
- drops the PIE `snapshot`, resets selection (`coord`);
- **fresh `CommandBus`** so an old-world undo/redo can't replay against the new kernel world (Codex correction 1);
- **`glb_source_path = None`** — swapped-in world has no GLB source — preserving `reload_hook`/`open_dialog` (Codex correction 2).

Preserves the GPU context, camera, `PlayState`, audit ledger, tick counter. `notify`-watcher teardown stays the binary's concern (dispatch 2, reacting to the now-`None` `glb_source_path()`).

## Scope / invariants

- **Zero binary changes** — `editor/rge-editor/**` untouched (Rule-8 verified). No `open_request.rs` / `asset_reload.rs` / `rge-scene-loader` / `rge-data` / `runtime-headless` edits. Only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/{mod,tests}.rs` (+220, single crate).
- `notify` / `rfd` remain out of editor-shell.

## Tests

6 headless unit tests (`replace_world_*`): new-world install + non-CAD mode; CAD-mode clear; render-mesh blank; Editing-gate rejection (no mutation on error); CommandBus dirty→clean reset; glb_source clear with loader hook preserved.

## Verification

Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` — single run, **`VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`** (`VERIFY_EXIT=0`): fmt + 9 architecture lints + lint-test suite + deny + workspace tests (incl. the 6 new) + doctests + bench-no-run. Step-0 worktree-cache self-heal fired.

## Follow-up (dispatch 2)

Wire `Ctrl+O` to accept `.rge-scene` → `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_world_from_path` → `replace_world`, with the binary tearing down the GLB watcher off the cleared `glb_source_path` (the #263/#264 watcher-shutdown caveat lands there).

title: SCENE-WORLD-BRIDGE: promote scene path resolver into rge-scene-loader
body:
## What

Promote the editor-binary-private scene **path resolver** (`load_scene_world_from_path`) into the production `rge-scene-loader` crate — the **Scene→World bridge** substrate that unblocks in-app scene Open (a follow-up). Dispatch `SCENE-WORLD-BRIDGE`; Codex pre-execution review **APPROVED**.

## Why

`rge_scene_loader::load_scene_into_world` (Scene→World) was already production, but the surrounding **path → Scene → World** resolution (`.rge-project`/`.rge-scene` extension dispatch + project first-scene resolution + file read + RON parse) lived as a **private** fn in `rge-editor`'s `main.rs`, reachable only from the `--scene` startup branch. In-app scene Open needs it as a reusable, tested production API.

## How

- **`rge-scene-loader/src/lib.rs`** — add `pub fn load_scene_world_from_path(&Path) -> Result<World, SceneWorldLoadError>` + a CLI-neutral thiserror `SceneWorldLoadError` that wraps the existing `SceneLoadError` via a `Loader(_)` variant. The loader's error boundary is **preserved, not broadened**; `load_scene_into_world` + `SceneLoadError` are byte-for-byte unchanged.
- **`rge-editor/src/main.rs`** — delete the local `SceneLoadCliError` + `Display` + `load_scene_world_from_path`; the `--scene` branch now calls the new API (same one-line-stderr + `ExitCode::FAILURE` posture). Drop the now-unused `rge-data` + `ron` deps. Refresh two stale doc comments.
- **Tests** move to `crates/rge-scene-loader/tests/scene_path_loader.rs` (golden-project success + unsupported-extension).

## Scope / invariants

- `crates/editor-shell`, `runtime/runtime-headless`, and `crates/rge-data` are **untouched** (single-crate-pair change + the new test). The rge-data identity-only test is a schema-local test, **not** a duplicate of the production loader.
- Only observable text delta: the `UnsupportedExtension` message drops its redundant `--scene ` self-prefix (the binary wrapper already prints `failed to load --scene {path}:`).
- Runtime-headless dedupe + in-app scene Open are explicit **follow-ups** (the latter will need explicit watcher-shutdown semantics per the #263 review caveat).

## Verification

Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` — single run, **`VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`** (`VERIFY_EXIT=0`): fmt + 9 architecture lints + lint-test suite + deny + workspace tests (incl. the 2 moved tests, both pass) + doctests + bench-no-run. Step-0 worktree-cache self-heal fired (linked worktree).

title: ai-dispatch: GLB watcher follows in-app Open (ISSUE-258 follow-up)
body:
## What

Make the binary-owned GLB hot-reload watcher follow an in-app **Ctrl+O Open** — the smallest unfinished edge of the in-app Open-GLB feature (ISSUE-258, #258).

## Why

ISSUE-258 added Ctrl+O in-app Open, which loads + swaps a user-picked GLB and commits its path as the editor-shell's new `glb_source_path`. But the `notify`-backed `GlbWatcher` is binary-owned and stayed rooted on the original `--glb` launch directory, so automatic hot-reload kept watching the **old** file. This closes that gap.

## How

All changes are in `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` (one file, +185/−2):

- **`glb_watcher_reroot_target(watched, source) -> Option<PathBuf>`** — pure, side-effect-free re-root decision (the unit-tested core):
  - `source == None` → `None` (nothing to follow; existing watcher left as-is)
  - `source == Some(p)` and `watched == Some(p)` → `None` (steady state — no per-frame churn)
  - otherwise → `Some(p)` (first-root from the demo, or re-root onto a different file)
- **`EditorApp.watched_glb_path`** — records the last path handed to `GlbWatcher::new`, set on **every** attempt (Ok or Err) so a persistently-unwatchable source can't rebuild the watcher each frame.
- **`EditorApp::sync_glb_watcher`** — performs only the `notify` side effects; on `Err` it drops the stale watcher (so it can't reload the old file) and warns (manual `R` still works). Invoked after `shell.window_event`, so an Open re-roots the watcher the same frame.

## Scope / invariants

- `notify` and `rfd` stay entirely inside the `rge-editor` binary.
- `crates/editor-shell` and `editor/rge-editor/src/glb_watcher.rs` are **untouched** (single-file diff).
- The watcher still only drives the existing `handle_asset_reload` hook — no new editor-shell surface.

## Tests

4 headless unit tests (`reroot_target_*`) pin the full truth table — no winit loop, no real `notify` watcher, plain `PathBuf` literals.

## Verification

Canonical gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` — single coherent run, `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.` (`VERIFY_EXIT=0`): fmt + 9 architecture lints + lint-test suite + deny + workspace tests (incl. the 4 new tests) + doctests + bench-no-run. Step-0 worktree-cache self-heal fired (linked worktree).
