title: ai-dispatch ISSUE-177: Wire runtime-headless to rge-scene-loader
body:
Implements ISSUE-177 / task #46: `runtime-headless` becomes the first non-test consumer of `rge-scene-loader`.

Changes:

- Adds direct runtime-headless deps on `rge-data`, `rge-scene-loader`, and workspace `ron`.
- Replaces the stub binary with one-position `<project-path>` parsing.
- Parses `rge_data::Project`, resolves the first scene relative to the manifest directory, parses `rge_data::Scene`, loads via `rge_scene_loader::load_scene_into_world`, advances one `World` tick, and prints entity/tick evidence.
- Adds one integration test file that invokes the binary against `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` and covers CLI rejection paths.

Review note:

- The filed issue body expected `current_tick=1`, but Claude plan gate rev0 correctly rejected that as unreachable: `World::new()` starts at `current_tick() == 1`, and the required single `advance_tick()` makes the kernel tick `2`. The approved TASK rev1 pins `current_tick=2`; Codex control passed that branch.

Verification from dispatch:

- `cargo build -p rge-runtime-headless`
- `cargo run -q -p rge-runtime-headless -- golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project`
- `cargo test -p rge-runtime-headless --no-fail-fast`
- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all`
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`

Closes #177.

title: Decide typed ComponentValue scene bridge ownership and mapping strategy
body:
## Context

PR #164 landed the read-only preflight audit for #163. The EXEC packet answered Q4 = NO and Q5 = NEEDS_HUMAN: current code does not justify a safe implementation dispatch for typed `ComponentValue` loading yet.

Task #38 already pinned the simple-scene file-format shape:

- `ComponentValue.type_id` strings use the rge-data scene-envelope convention (`rge::components::Transform`, `rge::components::Camera`, `rge::components::Light`, `rge::components::Visibility`).
- Camera has explicit `Visibility::Visible`.
- Camera perspective FOV uses `fov_y_radians: 1.0471976`.

Task #39 then audited the runtime bridge and found three load-bearing gaps before source work is safe.

## Decisions Needed

1. **Bridge owner and dependency direction**

   Choose where the `rge_data::Scene` / `ComponentValue { type_id, data }` -> `rge_kernel_ecs::World` typed bridge should live.

   Candidate directions from the audit:

   - New Tier-2 loader crate, e.g. `rge-scene-loader`, depending on `rge-data`, `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-kernel-types`, and component crates.
   - Runtime-owned bridge inside runtime crates.
   - Editor-owned bridge through `crates/editor-shell` / editor integration.
   - Another explicit owner.

   The audit rules out ownership in `rge-data`, kernel crates, renderer crates, and `script-host` without a new architecture decision.

2. **Type-id mapping mechanism**

   Choose how canonical schema strings map to concrete Rust component types.

   Candidate directions from the audit:

   - Explicit match table in the bridge owner: `rge::components::Transform` -> `rge_components_spatial::Transform`, etc.
   - Reflect-driven mapping, which would require adding `#[derive(Reflect)]` or equivalent and deciding how `FQ_TYPE_NAME` aligns with the schema strings.
   - Hand-rolled typed `from_ron::<C>` dispatch per supported component.
   - Another approved design that respects `kernel/types`' no-global-runtime-registry rule.

3. **ECS component attachment strategy**

   Choose how scene component types become attachable to `rge_kernel_ecs::World`.

   Candidate directions from the audit:

   - Add `impl rge_kernel_ecs::Component` in the owning component crates (`rge-components-spatial`, `rge-components-render`, `rge-components-visibility`).
   - Add `SnapshotComponent` registration where needed.
   - Use wrapper types in the bridge owner.
   - Use a different attachment strategy.

## Recommended Resolution

Default recommendation unless you override it:

- Create a new Tier-2 `rge-scene-loader` crate as the bridge owner.
- Use an explicit match table for the currently supported schema strings.
- Add `impl rge_kernel_ecs::Component` for `Transform`, `Camera`, `Light`, and `Visibility` in their owning component crates.
- Do not use a runtime global registry.
- Defer `Reflect`-driven loading until the derive macro and schema-string convention are intentionally aligned.

After this is decided, the next implementation dispatch can be scoped narrowly around the chosen owner and only the four simple-scene component types.

title: ai-dispatch ISSUE-161: Add typed payloads to golden simple-scene
body:
## Summary

Implements the bounded fixture + schema-only test follow-up for the typed-payload arbiter decision recorded in #160 (closes #158's `NEEDS_HUMAN` audit).

- `golden-projects/simple-scene/scenes/main.rge-scene` gains typed `ComponentValue` payloads on the existing Camera entity (Transform + Camera + Visibility) and a new KeyLight entity (Transform + Light).
- `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` adds three new tests pinning per-entity `ComponentValue` ordering, verbatim `type_id` strings, byte-exact raw `data` strings, and `ron::Value` shape inspection. No component crate is pulled into `rge-data`'s dev-deps.
- All four arbiter-approved canonical `type_id` strings are baked in: `"rge::components::Transform"`, `"rge::components::Camera"`, `"rge::components::Light"`, `"rge::components::Visibility"`.
- Camera carries `Visibility::Visible` explicitly; Camera projection pins `fov_y_radians: 1.0471976` (FRAC_PI_3 f32 spelling).

## Verification (executor-run)

- `cargo test -p rge-data --test golden_simple_scene_schema` — exit 0 (7 passed, 0 failed)
- `cargo test -p rge-data --test golden_simple_scene_load_tick` — exit 0 (load+tick regression still green after the entity count flip from 1 to 2)
- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` — exit 0, all 7 steps PASS (fmt / arch-lints / arch-lint tests / cargo-deny / cargo test --workspace --all-targets / cargo test --doc / bench --no-run)
- Codex control verdict: **pass** · commit_readiness: **ready_for_publish**

## Deviations from the brief (reviewer-on-merge note)

Codex translated three minor points at the TASK-packet step; each is acceptable:

1. **Light entity named `KeyLight`** (brief said `Light`) — semantic improvement for a directional key light; not load-bearing.
2. **Light has 2 components, no `Visibility`** (brief said 3 including Visibility) — Codex correctly interpreted issue #160's Q2 decision as scoped to the **camera** entity only. The Light entity using `Visibility::Inherited`'s default matches real-scene practice; only the camera pins explicit `Visible`.
3. **Camera `Camera` payload uses spaced RON** (brief had no-space form) — both round-trip identically; tests pin byte-exact strings so the fixture is internally consistent.

## Open question from executor (non-blocking)

`ron 0.12.1` parses unsuffixed floats through `ron::Value` as `Number::F64` rather than `Number::F32`, even though `1.0471976` is intended as an f32 literal. The new `expect_float` helper widens F32→f64 and asserts against `1.047_197_6_f64` / `100_000.0_f64`. The f32 *intent* is still pinned because the raw payload string is asserted byte-for-byte and the substring `"fov_y_radians: 1.0471976"` is required to appear inside `"Perspective("`. Explicit f32-variant pinning is not possible without pulling a component crate into dev-deps.

## Test plan

- [x] Schema test exits 0 with 7 tests (4 existing + 3 new)
- [x] Load+tick regression still green after entity-count change
- [x] Canonical 7-step verify gate green
- [x] Diff stat: only the 2 allowed source-facing files + this dispatch's own handoff/log artifacts
- [x] No `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` changes
- [x] No component crate imported by the schema test
- [x] All four arbiter-approved `type_id` strings present verbatim

Closes #161.

title: Decide ComponentValue.type_id convention for typed simple-scene payloads
body:
## Context

ISSUE-158 / PR #159 recovered a read-only audit for typed component payloads in `golden-projects/simple-scene`.

The audit found exact candidate RON payload strings for `Transform`, `Camera`, `Light`, and `Visibility`, but only the `Transform` `ComponentValue.type_id` string is currently proven by repo fixtures:

- Proven: `"rge::components::Transform"`
- Candidate envelope convention:
  - `"rge::components::Camera"`
  - `"rge::components::Light"`
  - `"rge::components::Visibility"`
- Candidate Rust module-path convention:
  - `"rge_components_render::camera::Camera"`
  - `"rge_components_render::light::Light"`
  - `"rge_components_visibility::visibility::Visibility"`
- Future option: wait for `#[derive(Reflect)]` emitted `FQ_TYPE_NAME` / real `TYPE_ID` conventions.

## Decision Needed

Choose the canonical `ComponentValue.type_id` convention for raw scene envelopes before any golden simple-scene typed-component fixture is edited.

## Questions

1. Should simple-scene use rge-data envelope convention strings, Rust fully-qualified module paths, or wait for Reflect-backed names?
2. Should `Visibility` be explicit as `Visible` on the camera entity, or omitted until a future light/mesh entity needs it?
3. If using the envelope convention, should `Camera` pin `fov_y_radians: 1.0471976` for the schema-only fixture?

## Recommendation To Decide

Prefer the current rge-data envelope convention for schema fixtures:
`"rge::components::Camera"`, `"rge::components::Light"`, `"rge::components::Visibility"`.

Record that this is a scene-envelope convention, not a Rust module path. Reflect-backed runtime identity can supersede or bridge it later through an explicit migration.

## After Decision

Only then queue the next dispatch: add typed raw `ComponentValue` payloads to `golden-projects/simple-scene/scenes/main.rge-scene` and extend `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` with schema-only assertions.

That is the best next task because it resolves the one real blocker without letting the executor guess a convention.

## Diff scope

- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs` — new `EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault` variant + Digit0 Ctrl-without-Shift mapping + handler delegating to `set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)` (+9 lines)
- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` — Digit0 mapping coverage (+19 lines)
- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs` — fresh-shell no-op, Ctrl+2→Ctrl+0 reset-and-undo, coalesce-window distinct-entry coverage (+102 lines)
- Plus standard dispatch metadata: TASK/EXEC packets + audit log

```
Verification: pass (exit 0)
Codex control verdict: pass
Commit readiness: ready_for_publish
```

All 7 dispatch.verify.ps1 steps green: fmt, architecture lints, architecture-lint tests, cargo deny, workspace all-targets tests, workspace doctests, bench --no-run.

## Test plan

- [x] Digit0 with Ctrl (without Shift) maps to ResetTimeScaleDefault
- [x] Fresh-shell Ctrl+0 is a no-op (default == default), does not push stack entry, does not flip dirty flag
- [x] Ctrl+2 then Ctrl+0 outside the 500ms coalesce window pushes two distinct stack entries
- [x] Undo from Ctrl+0 restores TimeScale to 2.0x

| Q | Finding |
|---|---------|
| **Q1 boundary** | First red is `2026-05-11T15:25:43Z` (run `25679615476`, sha `517aa88`), not `2026-05-22`. `058e26d` was last-green only for Format / Architecture; Tests / cargo-deny weren't green before either; `bench.yml` was added later. 200/200 runs in the recent window concluded `failure`. |
| **Q2 error pattern** | Identical across all five workflows: job queued → `if: success()` true → "Job is about to start running on the hosted runner: GitHub Actions \<id\>" → nothing. No `Set up job`, no checkout, no toolchain install, no step output. `system.txt` is 8 lines per job; per-job `/logs` returns HTTP 404 `BlobNotFound`; `gh run view --log-failed` returns `log not found`. Wall time 2–3 s per job. |
| **Q3 category** | All five workflows: **infrastructure / hosted-runner startup, pre-step**. `steps: []`, `runner_name: ""` — runner allocated, never executed any step. This is upstream of the repo / workflows entirely. |
| **Q4 parity gap** | `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` mirrors `fmt.yml`, `architecture.yml`, `deny.yml`, `tests.yml` — but **not** `bench.yml` (`cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`). The script docstring claims "four workflows one-for-one" which is literally accurate; the repo has five in-scope workflows now. Real verification blind spot. |
| **Q5 smallest follow-up** | **Human-owned GitHub Actions account-level investigation** — admin checks Actions billing / spending limit / hosted-runner availability at the org and repo Actions settings. **Zero in-repo edits.** Any in-repo follow-up (mirror `bench.yml` in `dispatch.verify.ps1`, etc.) is strictly larger and does not fix the failure boundary. |

## Review caveats

**ZIP-on-disk during audit.** The TASK packet's `MAY use read-only gh api / gh run view / gh workflow commands` clause says "Do not download artifacts or write logs to disk." The executor wrote 5 ZIP files (`fmt.zip`, `bench.zip`, `arch.zip`, `deny.zip`, `tests.zip`) via `gh api .../logs > *.zip` and used `unzip -p` to extract `system.txt` content. The executor's reasoning (visible in the Verification Results section):
- Per-job `/logs` endpoint returned `HTTP 404 BlobNotFound` for every job
- `gh run view --log-failed` returned `log not found`
- Run-level ZIP at `/actions/runs/<run-id>/logs` was the only available log surface

The ZIPs are NOT in the diff, NOT in the tracked tree, NOT in untracked working-tree state — executor cleaned up after extraction. Codex control accepted as `pass`. Substantive content is unaffected. Spirit of the constraint (no log artifacts polluting the repo) was fully honored; the literal text (no disk writes) was violated by necessity to answer Q2.

**Suggested doctrine refinement** (not part of this PR): future read-only audit task briefs should clarify the constraint as "no log artifacts in committed or tracked files" rather than "no disk writes," which would explicitly allow ephemeral OS-temp downloads as long as the worktree stays clean.

## Test plan
- [x] Diff stat: 5 new files in `ai_handoffs/` + `ai_dispatch_logs/`; zero source/workflow/Cargo edits
- [x] EXEC report has the exact `## 5-Question CI Preflight Answer Block` heading with Q1–Q5 sub-headings
- [x] EXEC footer is `HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE` / `NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI` / `EXIT_CODE: 0`
- [x] All seven verbatim review-gate MUSTs from task #8 quoted in TASK packet
- [x] No ZIP / temp / log artifacts in diff or working tree (confirmed via `git ls-files | grep zip` + `git status` + `find . -name "*.zip"`)
- [x] Workspace stays clean — no other tracked files modified

Closes #100.

title: feat(io-gltf): opt-in AssetStoreCache adapter forwarding through dyn rge_asset_store::Cache
body:
## Summary

Closes #94. Lands the opt-in `AssetStoreCache` adapter in `rge-io-gltf`
specified by the #92 audit's Q5: implements the typed
`crate::cache_stub::Cache` trait by forwarding storage through a
caller-supplied `Box<dyn rge_asset_store::Cache>`. The existing
`MemoryCache` impl remains the default for every editor, loader,
importer, exporter, and integration-test call site. Callers that
want persistent / asset-store-backed caching opt in by name.

## What lands

**NEW** — `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` (+574 lines):
- `pub struct AssetStoreCache` holding `Box<dyn ByteCache>` + five
  typed `HashMap`s mirroring each asset family
  (`meshes`, `materials`, `animations`, `skeletons`, `images`).
- `impl Cache for AssetStoreCache` — best-effort write-through to
  the byte backing (`drop(self.backing.put(...))`) preserves the
  trait's infallible contract while still exercising the
  asset-store seam.
- Fallible `try_insert_*` family — returns `Result<_, GltfError>`
  for callers that need to detect backing I/O failures. Mirror is
  NOT updated when the backing write fails (clean failure-mode
  semantics).
- Per-family `asset_id_for_*` helpers returning
  `AssetId::from_raw(handle.0)` — the canonical byte encoding is
  chosen so the BLAKE3 hash of the bytes matches the existing
  `content_hash()` digest, so the typed handle and the asset-store
  `AssetId` share the same BLAKE3 key.
- Custom canonical byte encoders per asset family (preferred over
  RON/postcard/bincode the task allowed, because aligning the
  canonical-bytes hash with the existing content-hash avoids a
  second hash dimension).

**EDIT** — `crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs` (+13/−3):
- `mod asset_store_cache;` declaration.
- `pub use asset_store_cache::AssetStoreCache;` re-export.
- New `GltfError::Cache(String)` variant with `#[error("cache: {0}")]`
  thiserror annotation. Additive on the existing `#[non_exhaustive]`
  enum so downstream match-on-error sites stay compiling.
- Stub-comment block updated to note that W16 is reached via the
  opt-in adapter rather than replacing `cache_stub`.

**EDIT** — `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs` (+27/−9): comment-only
softening of the migration promise per the #92 audit Q5 (the file
is no longer slated for deletion; `crate::Cache` is no longer
slated to become a re-export of `rge_asset_store::Cache`). No API
change.

**EDIT** — `crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml` (+6 lines): single dep edge
`rge-asset-store = { path = "../asset-store" }`. No workspace
manifest changes. Cargo.lock delta is **exactly one line**:

```diff
 dependencies = [
   "blake3",
   "gltf",
+  "rge-asset-store",
   "rge-io-image",
   "ron",
   "serde",
```

## Test plan

All three audit-Q5 assertions covered by `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`:

- [x] **(a) Round-trip equality** —
  `insert_mesh_returns_same_handle_as_memory_cache`: the adapter
  produces the same content-hash handle as `MemoryCache` for the
  same `MeshAsset`; `get_mesh` returns the same typed value.
- [x] **(b) BLAKE3 dedup preserved** —
  `duplicate_inserts_yield_one_asset_store_entry`: two inserts of
  the same mesh result in `backing.len() == 1` (dedup happens
  through the asset-store backing, not just the typed mirror).
  `re_inserting_same_mesh_yields_stable_handle` confirms typed-
  mirror parity. `distinct_meshes_get_distinct_asset_ids` is the
  negative-dedup case.
- [x] **(c) Cache-error surfacing** —
  `try_insert_mesh_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache` + the
  image variant + `cache_error_bad_asset_id_maps_to_gltf_cache_variant`:
  all backing failures surface as `GltfError::Cache(_)`, and the
  typed mirror is NOT touched on failure.

Mechanical gates (all reported pass by Codex control):
- [x] `cargo build -p rge-io-gltf` → exit 0
- [x] `cargo test -p rge-io-gltf --lib --no-fail-fast` → exit 0
- [x] `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0
  (new io-gltf → asset-store edge clears all 10 architecture lints)
- [x] `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → all 6 steps PASS, exit 0
- [x] Codex control verdict: `pass`,
  `commit_readiness: ready_for_publish`

## Scope verification (all 5 verbatim MUST clauses)

- [x] **MUST be an opt-in adapter inside rge-io-gltf, not a trait re-export** —
  `crate::Cache` and `crate::MemoryCache` unchanged. `AssetStoreCache`
  is a second `impl Cache`, not a re-export.
- [x] **MUST leave the existing MemoryCache as the default for editor / tests / loaders** —
  Zero diffs in `editor/**`, `crates/editor-shell/**`. Every existing
  call site still uses `MemoryCache`.
- [x] **MUST NOT change the public trait method signatures of `crate::Cache` (`insert_*` / `get_*`)** —
  `cache_stub.rs` diff is comment-only.
- [x] **MUST NOT modify kernel/**, editor/**, crates/editor-shell/**, crates/io-image/**, or crates/asset-store/**** —
  Diff stat confirms zero files in any of those directories.
- [x] **MUST halt if Cargo.lock changes extend beyond the single new asset-store dep edge** —
  Cargo.lock delta is literally one line: `+ "rge-asset-store",`
  under `rge-io-gltf`'s dependency list. No new packages, no
  version changes.

title: audit: W16 rge-asset-store integration shape — task #4 preflight (salvaged)
body:
## Summary

Closes #92. Lands the W16 `rge-asset-store` integration shape audit
content — the read-only preflight that task #4 in
`.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` was designed to produce. The audit itself
executed cleanly (the `ISSUE-92_EXEC_2026-05-22_16-52-05+0300.md`
packet has the full 5-question answer block, ~485 lines). The PR is
labelled "salvaged" because the autonomous dispatch did not auto-
publish through the canonical path — it was blocked by an unrelated
verify-gate regression in `rge-gfx --lib` (now fixed on main in
`a533b48`).

## What's in the audit (EXEC packet `16:52:05`)

The 5-question answer block covers, with file:line refs throughout:

1. **Cache surfaces today.** Three distinct surfaces enumerated —
   typed `rge_io_gltf::Cache`, byte-oriented unused
   `rge_io_image::asset_store_stub::Cache`, and
   `rge_asset_store::Cache` (`InMemoryCache` + `LocalCache`).
2. **First target.** `rge-io-gltf` — only io-* crate where the cache
   surface has real production consumers (importer, exporter,
   scene-builder, editor binary, 11 integration tests).
3. **Shape mismatch.** Six concrete deltas block a direct trait
   re-export (the future the `cache_stub.rs:11-12` migration comment
   promises): typed-family vs byte-oriented, borrow vs owned,
   infallible vs `Result`, handle shape, serialization boundary,
   LRU/persistence semantics. The audit concludes an **adapter is
   required, not a re-export**.
4. **kernel/asset-view consumer pressure.** No — asset-view does NOT
   become a real consumer from this swap alone. Evidence cites its
   v0 NON-GOALS block and the cache-vs-asset-view demarcation in
   `crates/asset-store/src/cache.rs:14-18`.
5. **Smallest follow-up dispatch.** Opt-in adapter at
   `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` forwarding through
   `dyn rge_asset_store::Cache`. Allowed-file surface enumerated,
   verification gates named, halt conditions listed. Zero kernel
   crate touches.

## Why this dispatch was salvaged via PR rather than auto-published

Task #4 is the first read-only preflight task the autonomous
dispatch loop has run. Its execution exposed an interaction worth
recording:

1. **Round 0 audit** produced the EXEC packet above — clean, on-scope,
   read-only.
2. **Verify gate** then ran (the orchestrator runs `dispatch.verify.ps1`
   after every Claude execute, regardless of dispatch type). It
   caught `STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION (0xc0000005)` in
   `cargo test -p rge-gfx --lib` — a regression unrelated to the
   audit's scope.
3. **Auto-routed CORRECTION packet** asked Claude to fix the
   unrelated rge-gfx --lib crash, expanding the dispatch's scope
   beyond the read-only audit intent.
4. **Scope-preserving halt clause** (added in `27f6e1b` to task #4
   in `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`) correctly bounced this: Claude wrote
   `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN` / `EXEC_STATUS: blocked` in the
   `ISSUE-92_EXEC_2026-05-22_17-00-51+0300.md` packet rather than
   accepting the source-fix correction.
5. **Manual salvage** lands the audit content via this PR. The
   rge-gfx --lib stabilization (`GPU_TEST_LOCK` parallel for the gfx
   crate) was committed separately on main as `a533b48` — narrow,
   test-only, no scope drift.

This dispatch is therefore a **positive validation** of the halt
clause: the system correctly refused to coerce a read-only audit
into a source-fix dispatch, and the audit content + the halt
artifacts together form the canonical task #4 record.

## What lands

Nine files, +1052 insertions, all under `ai_handoffs/` and
`ai_dispatch_logs/`. Zero source-code or workspace-manifest
changes. Verify gate confirmed green on `main` BEFORE the
rebase (post `a533b48`), and the rebased branch tip is just
markdown additions — verify-trivial.

- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_TASK_2026-05-22_16-45-11+0300.md` (+ meta)
- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_CORRECT_2026-05-22_16-56-14+0300.md` (+ meta)
- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_2026-05-22_16-52-05+0300.md` (+ meta) — the audit
- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-92_EXEC_2026-05-22_17-00-51+0300.md` (+ meta) — the halt
- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-22_17-01-46+0300.md` — full queue audit log

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test -p rge-gfx --lib --no-fail-fast` → 180 passed, exit 0 (on `a533b48`)
- [x] `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → all 6 steps PASS, exit 0 (on `a533b48`)
- [x] Audit content is on-scope (read-only): no `crates/`,
  `kernel/`, `editor/`, `Cargo.*` diffs in this PR.
- [x] Halt clause functioned correctly (round 1 EXEC packet shape
  matches the `EXEC_STATUS: blocked` + `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`
  contract).

## Follow-ups (separate dispatches)

Per the audit's Q5: smallest follow-up dispatch is an opt-in
`crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` adapter. The audit
specifies allowed-file surface, verification gates, and halt
conditions for that task. To be added to `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
as a new task entry after #92 closes.

title: test(editor): malformed-GLB reload retains prior frame, recovers via same hook
body:
## Summary

Closes #89. Adds the malformed-GLB reload regression coverage that
parallels the existing
`r_key_reload_on_missing_file_preserves_prior_frame` test — but
exercises the parser-failure path (file present, wrong magic) instead
of the missing-file path, AND proves the hook stays usable after the
failure by recovering through a valid overwrite of the same path.

## What lands

One new test inside `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`'s
`#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block:
`r_key_reload_on_malformed_glb_retains_then_recovers` (~179 lines).

Three-leg structure:

1. **Frame 1 — pre-reload**: build a shell from `cube.glb`, render,
   assert the central-row blue-dominant signature and
   `cube_pixel_count > 8`.
2. **Frame 2 — after malformed reload**: stage a temp file with
   wrong-magic bytes `b"not a glb"`, assert
   `GlbLoaderHook.reload_glb(&path).is_err()` directly (runs even on
   no-GPU CI — failure-mode coverage is environment-independent),
   attach the path through the production hook, call
   `shell.handle_asset_reload()`, render. Assert: signature still
   blue-dominant; per-channel avg RGB matches frame 1 within
   `CUBE_THRESHOLD`.
3. **Frame 3 — non-poison recovery**: overwrite the SAME temp path
   with valid `textured_uv_cube.glb` bytes, drive
   `shell.handle_asset_reload()` again through the same attached
   source, render. Assert: checkerboard color variance
   (`red_spread > 50 || blue_spread > 50`) AND at least one channel
   of avg RGB shifts by more than `CUBE_THRESHOLD` vs frames 1/2 —
   proving the failed parse did not poison the watcher/hook path.

Best-effort cleanup of the temp file/dir at the end; leaks across
panic are tolerable (temp dir is per-PID + per-nanosecond, so
parallel runs do not collide).

## Verbatim review-gate strings (per task #3 brief)

Both MUST clauses are honored by the test code:

- `MUST retain the prior rendered frame after the malformed reload`
  → frame-2 vs frame-1 per-channel avg RGB delta ≤ `CUBE_THRESHOLD`.
- `MUST follow the failed reload with a valid reload that proves the hook still works`
  → frame 3 produced via same attached path after valid overwrite,
  asserting checkerboard variance and >`CUBE_THRESHOLD` shift.

## Scope

All changes are test-only. Verified zero diffs in:

- shaders / WGSL / render pipelines / vertex layout / material API /
  lighting model / camera API / draw-path production logic

title: feat(io-gltf): M3 smooth-normal visual acceptance via sphere fixture
body:
## Summary

Closes #87. Adds visual acceptance coverage for the M3 imported-normal
path: a committed UV-sphere GLB fixture with smooth per-vertex normals,
a property test pinning the substrate invariants, and a headless
pixel/readback test asserting a driver-noise-resistant numeric delta
between the imported-NORMAL path and a same-geometry flat-recompute
construction.

The pre-existing `load_all_glb_meshes_preserves_input_normals_when_present`
test only proved imported normals reach the `RenderMesh` byte-for-byte —
because the cube fixture's NORMAL accessor happened to equal flat
recompute, it could not visibly distinguish the two paths. This PR
closes that gap.

## What lands

- `crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb` (binary,
  10528 bytes) -- the committed UV-sphere fixture. 12 stacks x 18
  slices, ~218 vertices, per-vertex normals = normalized radial
  vectors. Triangles are sorted back-to-front along the editor's
  (1,1,1) iso-camera direction so the visible hemisphere wins the
  overdraw race under the editor's `depth_write_enabled: false`
  lit-mesh pipeline. The sort is fixture-side only and is documented
  in `make_smooth_normal_sphere_glb`'s doc-comment.
- `crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs` -- property
  test asserting: exactly one drawable mesh; per-vertex normals 1:1
  with positions before render-mesh vertex tripling; unit-length
  normals (`(len - 1.0).abs() < 1e-4`); at least one triangle with
  three per-vertex normals diverging by `max channel delta > 0.1`
  (~5.7 degrees) so smooth-vs-flat divergence is structurally
  guaranteed.
- `crates/io-gltf/tests/common/mod.rs` -- adds
  `smooth_normal_sphere_fixture_path()` +
  `make_smooth_normal_sphere_glb()` helpers (procedural, deterministic,
  materialise-once pattern mirroring the existing `cube` / `animated_character` fixtures).
- `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` -- new test
  `smooth_normal_sphere_imported_vs_flat_recompute_visibly_differs_end_to_end`
  inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block. Renders the
  same baked geometry twice (Path A: `load_all_glb_meshes` imported
  normals; Path B: `RenderMesh::from_buffers_with_attributes(..., None, None, None)`
  flat recompute), scans the central horizontal row, and asserts two
  gates:
  - `common_on_mesh_pixels > 8` (both renders hit the sphere on the
    central row in enough pixels to be meaningful)
  - `sum_abs_rgb_delta > 300` (driver-noise-resistant absolute floor,
    documented in the task packet)

## Scope

All changes live inside test/fixture surface. Verified zero diffs in:

- shaders / WGSL / render pipelines / vertex layout / material API /
  lighting model / camera API / draw-path production logic
- `crates/asset-store/**`
- `kernel/**`
- `crates/gfx/**`
- `crates/brep-render/**`
- `crates/editor-shell/**`
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock`

## Test plan

- [x] `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exits 0 across all 6 steps on the
  branch tip (Codex queue verification round 0: pass).
- [x] Codex control review verdict: `pass`
  (`dispatch-ISSUE-87/codex.control.round0.json`).
- [x] Property test passes (`cargo test -p rge-io-gltf smooth_normal`).
- [x] Visual test passes when a headless GPU adapter is available;
  graceful skip otherwise via `skip_if_fixture_missing` +
  `render_shell_one_frame` returning `Option`.
- [x] All three task-#2 verbatim MUST clauses present in the dispatched
  issue body (#87) character-for-character.

## Acknowledged workaround

The fixture sorts triangles back-to-front along the iso-camera direction
because the editor's current lit-mesh pipeline uses
`depth_write_enabled: false` — without the sort, back-facing triangles
would overdraw the visible hemisphere and the sphere would render as
ambient-only. The alternative (toggling depth-write) sits in production
render-pipeline scope which is explicitly MUST NOT for task #2, so the
sort stays inside the fixture. The choice is documented at the call
site. A future production-depth-state task can drop the sort.

title: feat(editor): automatic GLB hot-reload via notify watcher
body:
## Summary

Closes #85. Adds an automatic GLB hot-reload watcher to `rge-editor`,
layered on top of the manual R-key path (`4fc71af`). When the editor
is launched with `--glb <path>`, a `notify`-backed watcher rooted at
the active file's parent directory turns coalesced modify events into
reload **requests** -- the actual reload still goes through
`EditorShell::handle_asset_reload` (the same call the R-key uses), so
the watcher is a strict producer with no render-asset mutation
surface of its own.

- `editor/rge-editor/src/glb_watcher.rs` -- new module: ~200ms
  debounce, parent-dir non-recursive watch, `Modify` events filtered
  to the active source path, channel-drained non-blockingly.
- `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` -- `EditorApp` `ApplicationHandler`
  wrapper drains pending watcher requests **before** the inner shell
  processes `WindowEvent::RedrawRequested` and calls
  `handle_asset_reload` when one is ready. Default cuboid demo path
  leaves both reload sources as no-ops.
- `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml` -- adds `notify = { workspace = true }`
  as a crate-local edge (no new package or version introduced;
  `notify = "8"` was already a workspace member).

## Manual salvage (commits on top of the autonomous run)

The autonomous run was queue-marked `ai-dispatch-failed` because Codex
control flagged two blockers:

1. **Cargo.lock dependency-edge change** clashed with the task's
   "Cargo.lock must not change" clause. Explicit one-off human
   override for this PR: `notify` was already a workspace dep and the
   delta is a single `rge-editor` lockfile edge. Future
   watcher-adjacent tasks will carry a per-task permission clause for
   in-tree workspace-dep edges; the template stays strict.

2. **Bare-relative GLB path bug**: `GlbWatcher::new` resolved the
   directory to watch via `glb_source_path.parent().ok_or_else(...)`,
   but `Path::parent()` returns `Some("")` -- not `None` -- for bare
   filenames like `asset.glb`. The empty path then failed
   `watcher.watch("")` at the OS layer, silently disabling automatic
   reload (manual R-key still worked, which masked the bug).

   Salvage `e752d64`: extracted `watch_parent_for` as a pure helper
   that normalizes `Some("")` -> `.` while still erroring on a
   genuinely empty input; tabled the resolution matrix in the helper's
   doc-comment; added 5 tests covering the matrix and an end-to-end
   smoke that constructs a real platform watcher against the cwd.
   `8e200b3` applies nightly fmt to those new tests.

## Test plan

- [x] `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` green at branch tip -- all 6 steps PASS
  (~79s warm-cache). Log archived locally at
  `OLD/dispatch.verify.issue85-salvage-2.log`.
- [x] 10 pre-existing `glb_watcher` tests still pass.
- [x] 5 new tests pass:
  `watch_parent_for_bare_relative_resolves_to_dot`,
  `watch_parent_for_dot_slash_relative_resolves_to_dot`,
  `watch_parent_for_nested_relative_resolves_to_parent_dir`,
  `watch_parent_for_empty_path_errors`,
  `new_accepts_bare_relative_path_by_watching_cwd`.
- [x] 2 e2e watcher integration tests pass (failure-then-recovery
  through `handle_asset_reload`).

## Commits

- `b80f0fe` -- original autonomous dispatch (watcher module + main.rs
  wrapper + Cargo.toml dep + dispatch logs)
- `e752d64` -- bare-relative path fix + tests (manual salvage)
- `8e200b3` -- nightly fmt on new tests (manual salvage)

## Review checklist

- [x] Watcher is producer-only -- no render-asset mutation
- [x] All reloads go through `EditorShell::handle_asset_reload`
- [x] Bare-relative path fix present + tested
- [x] Verify gate green
- [x] No new package introduced; lockfile delta is a single edge

title: [ai-dispatch] Broaden #18: TransformOp label preservation + stale-test reconciliation
body:
Follow-up to failed dispatch #18. The `TransformOp` label-preserving implementation on branch `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-18` (commit `6807cb6`) is conceptually sound, but #18's scope only permitted editing `transform.rs` — leaving tests elsewhere that still assert the old "strips labels" contract failing, so #18 could never pass verification.

## Goal

`TransformOp` preserves tessellation face labels, with every affected test reconciled.

## MAY edit

- `crates/cad-core/src/operators/transform.rs` — keep the label-preserving implementation; commit `6807cb6` is the reference. Positions transform; indices clone unchanged; labeled input uses `Tessellation::with_labels(..., labels.to_vec())`; unlabeled input stays on `Tessellation::new`; `output_is_labeled(&[true])` becomes `true`.
- `crates/cad-core/src/graph/operator_graph.rs` (~line 795) and `crates/cad-core/tests/labeled_tessellation_pipeline.rs` (~lines 163, 303) — update these stale tests, which still assert the OLD strip-labels contract.
- `crates/cad-core/tests/topology_lineage_smoke.rs` (~line 106) — this failed during #18's verification but does not use `TransformOp` and passes on current `main`. Rerun it; change it ONLY if this branch proves a genuine intended expectation change — otherwise diagnose the failure, do not blind-update the assertion.

## Verification

Run the full `cargo test` suite. Note: `cargo` is not on the default PATH in this workspace — prepend `A:\RustCache\cargo\bin` (CARGO_HOME = `A:\RustCache\cargo`). The earlier #18 executor wrongly reported an "environment limitation" because of this; it is not a real limitation.

## Notes

- #18 itself stays `ai-dispatch-failed` as the record of the narrow-scope attempt; this issue supersedes it.
- Do not merge or publish `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-18` / `6807cb6` as-is.
